General guidelines.
   - When you execute commands, always save their logs to files for easier reading if the output becomes large.
   - When running commands, do not set a timeout yourself — let them run as long as needed (default timeout - 2 minutes is more than enough), and once they finish, review the logs in the file.
   - When running sudo commands (especially package installations like apt-get, yum, npm install, etc.), always run them in the background to avoid timeout issues and permission errors when the process needs to be killed. Use the run_in_background parameter or append & to the command.
   - When CI is failing or user reports failures, consider adding a detailed investigation protocol to your todo list with these steps:
      Step 1: List recent runs with timestamps using: gh run list --repo link-foundation/meta-theory --branch issue-21-9206faf90060 --limit 5 --json databaseId,conclusion,createdAt,headSha
      Step 2: Verify runs are after the latest commit by checking timestamps and SHA
      Step 3: For each non-passing run, download logs to preserve them: gh run view {run-id} --repo link-foundation/meta-theory --log > ci-logs/{workflow}-{run-id}.log
      Step 4: Read each downloaded log file using Read tool to understand the actual failures
      Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
      This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
      Note: If user says \"failing\" but tools show \"passing\", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.
   - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.
   - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.
   - When you create debug, test, or example/experiment scripts for fixing, always keep them in an ./examples and/or ./experiments folders so you can reuse them later.
   - When testing your assumptions, use the experiment scripts, and add it to ./experiments folder.
   - When your experiments can show real world use case of the software, add it to ./examples folder.
   - When you face something extremely hard, use divide and conquer — it always helps.

   - When you start, make sure you create detailed plan for yourself and follow your todo list step by step, make sure that as many points from these guidelines are added to your todo list to keep track of everything that can help you solve the issue with highest possible quality.

   - When you read issue, read all details and comments thoroughly.
   - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, download the image to a local file first, then use Read tool to view and analyze it. IMPORTANT: Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify the file is a valid image (not HTML). Use a CLI tool like 'file' command to check the actual file format. Reading corrupted or non-image files (like GitHub's \"Not Found\" pages saved as .png) can cause \"Could not process image\" errors and will crash the AI solver process. If the file command shows \"HTML\", \"text\", or \"ASCII text\", the download FAILED — do NOT call Read on this file. Instead: (1) For images from GitHub issues/PRs (URLs containing \"github.com/user-attachments\"), these require authentication — retry with: curl -L -H \"Authorization: token \$(gh auth token)\" -o <filename> \"<url>\" (2) If retry still fails, skip the image and note it was unavailable.
   - When you need issue details, use gh issue view https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-theory/issues/21.
   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner link-foundation [keywords].
   - When you need repo context, read files in your working directory.
   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.
   - When issue is not defined enough, write a comment to ask clarifying questions.
   - When accessing GitHub Gists (especially private ones), use gh gist view command instead of direct URL fetching to ensure proper authentication.
   - When you are fixing a bug, please make sure you first find the actual root cause, do as many experiments as needed.
   - When you are fixing a bug and code does not have enough tracing/logs, add them and make sure they stay in the code, but are switched off by default.
   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has THREE different comment types with different API endpoints:
      1. PR review comments (inline code comments): gh api repos/link-foundation/meta-theory/pulls/22/comments --paginate
      2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/link-foundation/meta-theory/issues/22/comments --paginate
      3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/link-foundation/meta-theory/pulls/22/reviews --paginate
      IMPORTANT: The command \"gh pr view --json comments\" ONLY returns conversation comments and misses review comments!
   - When you need latest comments on issue, use gh api repos/link-foundation/meta-theory/issues/21/comments --paginate.

Solution development and testing.
   - When issue is solvable, implement code with tests.
   - When implementing features, search for similar existing implementations in the codebase and use them as examples instead of implementing everything from scratch.
   - When coding, each atomic step that can be useful by itself should be commited to the pull request's branch, meaning if work will be interrupted by any reason parts of solution will still be kept intact and safe in pull request.
   - When you test:
      start from testing of small functions using separate scripts;
      write unit tests with mocks for easy and quick start.
   - When you test integrations, use existing framework.
   - When you test solution draft, include automated checks in pr.

   - When you see repeated test timeout patterns in CI, investigate the root cause rather than increasing timeouts.
   - When issue is unclear, write comment on issue asking questions.

   - When you open pr, describe solution draft and include tests.

GitHub CLI command patterns.
   - IMPORTANT: Always use --paginate flag when fetching lists from GitHub API to ensure all results are returned (GitHub returns max 30 per page by default).
   - When listing PR review comments (inline code comments), use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR conversation comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR reviews, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/reviews --paginate.
   - When listing issue comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When adding PR comment, use gh pr comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When filtering with jq, use gh api repos/\${owner}/\${repo}/pulls/\${prNumber}/comments --paginate --jq 'reverse | .[0:5]'.

Visual UI work and screenshots.
   - When you work on visual UI changes (frontend, CSS, HTML, design), include a render or screenshot of the final result in the pull request description.
   - When you need to show visual results, take a screenshot and save it to the repository (e.g., in a docs/screenshots/ or assets/ folder).
   - When you save screenshots to the repository, use permanent links in the pull request description markdown (e.g., https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-theory/blob/issue-21-9206faf90060/docs/screenshots/result.png?raw=true).
   - When uploading images, commit them to the branch first, then reference them using the GitHub blob URL format with ?raw=true suffix (works for both public and private repositories).
   - When the visual result is important for review, mention it explicitly in the pull request description with the embedded image." | jq -c .)

   - When you read issue, read all details and comments thoroughly.
   - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, download the image to a local file first, then use Read tool to view and analyze it. IMPORTANT: Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify the file is a valid image (not HTML). Use a CLI tool like 'file' command to check the actual file format. Reading corrupted or non-image files (like GitHub's "Not Found" pages saved as .png) can cause "Could not process image" errors and will crash the AI solver process. If the file command shows "HTML", "text", or "ASCII text", the download FAILED — do NOT call Read on this file. Instead: (1) For images from GitHub issues/PRs (URLs containing "github.com/user-attachments"), these require authentication — retry with: curl -L -H "Authorization: token $(gh auth token)" -o <filename> "<url>" (2) If retry still fails, skip the image and note it was unavailable.
   - When you need issue details, use gh issue view https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-theory/issues/21.
   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner link-foundation [keywords].
   - When you need repo context, read files in your working directory.
   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.
   - When issue is not defined enough, write a comment to ask clarifying questions.
   - When accessing GitHub Gists (especially private ones), use gh gist view command instead of direct URL fetching to ensure proper authentication.
   - When you are fixing a bug, please make sure you first find the actual root cause, do as many experiments as needed.
   - When you are fixing a bug and code does not have enough tracing/logs, add them and make sure they stay in the code, but are switched off by default.
   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has THREE different comment types with different API endpoints:
      1. PR review comments (inline code comments): gh api repos/link-foundation/meta-theory/pulls/22/comments --paginate
      2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/link-foundation/meta-theory/issues/22/comments --paginate
      3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/link-foundation/meta-theory/pulls/22/reviews --paginate
      IMPORTANT: The command "gh pr view --json comments" ONLY returns conversation comments and misses review comments!
   - When you need latest comments on issue, use gh api repos/link-foundation/meta-theory/issues/21/comments --paginate.

Solution development and testing.
   - When issue is solvable, implement code with tests.
   - When implementing features, search for similar existing implementations in the codebase and use them as examples instead of implementing everything from scratch.
   - When coding, each atomic step that can be useful by itself should be commited to the pull request's branch, meaning if work will be interrupted by any reason parts of solution will still be kept intact and safe in pull request.
   - When you test:
      start from testing of small functions using separate scripts;
      write unit tests with mocks for easy and quick start.
   - When you test integrations, use existing framework.
   - When you test solution draft, include automated checks in pr.
   - When you write or modify tests, consider setting reasonable timeouts at test, suite, and CI job levels so failures surface quickly instead of hanging.
   - When you see repeated test timeout patterns in CI, investigate the root cause rather than increasing timeouts.
   - When issue is unclear, write comment on issue asking questions.
   - When you encounter any problems that you unable to solve yourself (any human feedback or help), write a comment to the pull request asking for help.
   - When you need human help, use gh pr comment 22 --body "your message" to comment on existing PR.

Preparing pull request.
   - When you code, follow contributing guidelines.
   - When you commit, write clear message.
   - When you need examples of style, use gh pr list --repo link-foundation/meta-theory --state merged --search [keywords].
   - When you open pr, describe solution draft and include tests.
   - When there is a package with version and GitHub Actions workflows for automatic release, update the version (or other necessary release trigger) in your pull request to prepare for next release.
   - When you update existing pr 22, use gh pr edit to modify title and description.
   - When you are about to commit or push code, ALWAYS run local CI checks first if they are available in contributing guidelines (like ruff check, mypy, eslint, etc.) to catch errors before pushing.
   - When you finalize the pull request:
      follow style from merged prs for code, title, and description,
      make sure no uncommitted changes corresponding to the original requirements are left behind,
      make sure the default branch is merged to the pull request's branch,
      make sure all CI checks passing if they exist before you finish,
      check for latest comments on the issue and pull request to ensure no recent feedback was missed,
      double-check that all changes in the pull request answer to original requirements of the issue,
      make sure no new new bugs are introduced in pull request by carefully reading gh pr diff,
      make sure no previously existing features were removed without an explicit request from users via the issue description, issue comments, and/or pull request comments.
   - When you finish implementation, use gh pr ready 22.

Workflow and collaboration.
   - When you check branch, verify with git branch --show-current.
   - When you push, push only to branch issue-21-9206faf90060.
   - When you finish, create a pull request from branch issue-21-9206faf90060. (Note: PR 22 already exists, update it instead)
   - When you organize workflow, use pull requests instead of direct merges to default branch (main or master).
   - When you manage commits, preserve commit history for later analysis.
   - When you contribute, keep repository history forward-moving with regular commits, pushes, and reverts if needed.
   - When you face conflict that you cannot resolve yourself, ask for help.
   - When you collaborate, respect branch protections by working only on issue-21-9206faf90060.
   - When you mention result, include pull request url or comment url.
   - When you need to create pr, remember pr 22 already exists for this branch.

Self review.
   - When you check your solution draft, run all tests locally.
   - When you check your solution draft, verify git status shows a clean working tree with no uncommitted changes.
   - When you compare with repo style, use gh pr diff [number].
   - When you finalize, confirm code, tests, and description are consistent.

GitHub CLI command patterns.
   - IMPORTANT: Always use --paginate flag when fetching lists from GitHub API to ensure all results are returned (GitHub returns max 30 per page by default).
   - When listing PR review comments (inline code comments), use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR conversation comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR reviews, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/reviews --paginate.
   - When listing issue comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When adding PR comment, use gh pr comment NUMBER --body "text" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body "text" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When filtering with jq, use gh api repos/${owner}/${repo}/pulls/${prNumber}/comments --paginate --jq 'reverse | .[0:5]'.

Visual UI work and screenshots.
   - When you work on visual UI changes (frontend, CSS, HTML, design), include a render or screenshot of the final result in the pull request description.
   - When you need to show visual results, take a screenshot and save it to the repository (e.g., in a docs/screenshots/ or assets/ folder).
   - When you save screenshots to the repository, use permanent links in the pull request description markdown (e.g., https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-theory/blob/issue-21-9206faf90060/docs/screenshots/result.png?raw=true).
   - When uploading images, commit them to the branch first, then reference them using the GitHub blob URL format with ?raw=true suffix (works for both public and private repositories).
   - When the visual result is important for review, mention it explicitly in the pull request description with the embedded image.

Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36'\n82\t  });\n83\t  const page = await context.newPage();\n84\t\n85\t  // Navigate to the page\n86\t  await page.goto(article.url, {\n87\t    waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded',\n88\t    timeout: 120000\n89\t  });\n90\t\n91\t  // Wait for article body to appear\n92\t  await page.waitForSelector('.article-formatted-body', { timeout: 30000 });\n93\t\n94\t  // Scroll through the page to trigger lazy loading\n95\t  await page.evaluate(async () => {\n96\t    const scrollHeight = document.documentElement.scrollHeight;\n97\t    const viewportHeight = window.innerHeight;\n98\t    const scrollSteps = Math.ceil(scrollHeight / viewportHeight);\n99\t\n100\t    for (let i = 0; i < scrollSteps; i++) {\n101\t      window.scrollTo(0, i * viewportHeight);\n102\t      await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));\n103\t    }\n104\t    window.scrollTo(0, 0);\n105\t  });\n106\t\n107\t  // Wait for dynamic content\n108\t  await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n109\t\n110\t  // Extract article metadata from the page header area\n111\t  const metadata = await page.evaluate(() => {\n112\t    const meta = {};\n113\t\n114\t    // Author\n115\t    const authorEl = document.querySelector('.tm-user-info__username');\n116\t    if (authorEl) {\n117\t      meta.author = authorEl.innerText.trim();\n118\t      meta.authorUrl = authorEl.href || null;\n119\t    }\n120\t\n121\t    // Publication date\n122\t    const timeEl = document.querySelector('time[datetime]');\n123\t    if (timeEl) {\n124\t      meta.publishDate = timeEl.getAttribute('datetime');\n125\t      meta.publishDateText = timeEl.innerText.trim();\n126\t    }\n127\t\n128\t    // Reading time\n129\t    const readTimeEl = document.querySelector('.tm-article-reading-time__label');\n130\t    if (readTimeEl) {\n131\t      meta.readingTime = readTimeEl.innerText.trim();\n132\t    }\n133\t\n134\t    // Difficulty level\n135\t    const diffEl = document.querySelector('.tm-article-complexity__label');\n136\t    if (diffEl) {\n137\t      meta.difficulty = diffEl.innerText.trim();\n138\t    }\n139\t\n140\t    // Views\n141\t    const viewsEl = document.querySelector('.tm-icon-counter__value');\n142\t    if (viewsEl) {\n143\t      meta.views = viewsEl.getAttribute('title') || viewsEl.innerText.trim();\n144\t    }\n145\t\n146\t    // Hubs (use specific hub link selector to avoid duplicates)\n147\t    const hubEls = document.querySelectorAll('.tm-publication-hub__link');\n148\t    meta.hubs = Array.from(hubEls).map(el => {\n149\t      // Get only the first span text (hub name), not the asterisk\n150\t      const nameSpan = el.querySelector('span:first-child');\n151\t      return nameSpan ? nameSpan.innerText.trim() : el.innerText.trim().replace(/\\s*\\*\\s*$/, '');\n152\t    });\n153\t\n154\t    // Tags from meta keywords\n155\t    const keywordsMeta = document.querySelector('meta[name=\"keywords\"]');\n156\t    if (keywordsMeta) {\n157\t      const content = keywordsMeta.getAttribute('content');\n158\t      if (content) {\n159\t        meta.tags = content.split(',').map(t => t.trim()).filter(Boolean);\n160\t      }\n161\t    }\n162\t\n163\t    // Translation / original author info\n164\t    const originEl = document.querySelector('.tm-article-presenter__origin');\n165\t    if (originEl) {\n166\t      meta.originalAuthor = originEl.innerText.trim();\n167\t    }\n168\t\n169\t    // LD+JSON structured data for additional metadata\n170\t    const ldJsonScript = document.querySelector('script[type=\"application/ld+json\"]');\n171\t    if (ldJsonScript) {\n172\t      try {\n173\t        const ldData = JSON.parse(ldJsonScript.textContent);\n174\t        if (ldData.dateModified) meta.dateModified = ldData.dateModified;\n175\t        if (ldData.author?.name) meta.authorFullName = ldData.author.name;\n176\t      } catch (e) {\n177\t        // ignore parse errors\n178\t      }\n179\t    }\n180\t\n181\t    return meta;\n182\t  });\n183\t\n184\t  if (verbose) {\n185\t    console.log('   Metadata extracted:', JSON.stringify(metadata, null, 2));\n186\t  }\n187\t\n188\t  // Extract article content as structured data with HTML processing\n189\t  const content = await page.evaluate(() => {\n190\t    const articleBody = document.querySelector('.article-formatted-body');\n191\t    if (!articleBody) return null;\n192\t\n193\t    // Get article title\n194\t    const titleEl = document.querySelector('article h1');\n195\t    const title = titleEl ? titleEl.innerText.trim() : '';\n196\t\n197\t    /**\n198\t     * Convert an HTML element to markdown, preserving links and formatting\n199\t     */\n200\t    function nodeToMarkdown(node, context = {}) {\n201\t      if (node.nodeType === Node.TEXT_NODE) {\n202\t        return node.textContent;\n203\t      }\n204\t\n205\t      if (node.nodeType !== Node.ELEMENT_NODE) return '';\n206\t\n207\t      const tag = node.tagName.toLowerCase();\n208\t\n209\t      // Skip script, style, etc.\n210\t      if (['script', 'style', 'noscript', 'svg'].includes(tag)) return '';\n211\t\n212\t      // Handle links\n213\t      if (tag === 'a') {\n214\t        const href = node.getAttribute('href');\n215\t        const text = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n216\t        if (href && text) {\n217\t          return `[${text}](${href})`;\n218\t        }\n219\t        return text;\n220\t      }\n221\t\n222\t      // Handle bold\n223\t      if (tag === 'strong' || tag === 'b') {\n224\t        const text = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n225\t        return text ? `**${text}**` : '';\n226\t      }\n227\t\n228\t      // Handle italic\n229\t      if (tag === 'em' || tag === 'i') {\n230\t        const text = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n231\t        return text ? `*${text}*` : '';\n232\t      }\n233\t\n234\t      // Handle inline code\n235\t      if (tag === 'code' && !node.closest('pre')) {\n236\t        const text = node.textContent;\n237\t        return text ? `\\`${text}\\`` : '';\n238\t      }\n239\t\n240\t      // Handle line breaks\n241\t      if (tag === 'br') {\n242\t        return '\\n';\n243\t      }\n244\t\n245\t      // Handle subscript/superscript (common in math)\n246\t      if (tag === 'sub') {\n247\t        return `₍${node.textContent}₎`;\n248\t      }\n249\t      if (tag === 'sup') {\n250\t        return `^${node.textContent}`;\n251\t      }\n252\t\n253\t      // Handle formula images (Habr stores LaTeX in `source` attribute)\n254\t      if (tag === 'img' && node.classList.contains('formula')) {\n255\t        const source = node.getAttribute('source');\n256\t        if (source) {\n257\t          // Inline formula - wrap in single $\n258\t          return `$${source}$`;\n259\t        }\n260\t        // Fallback to alt text\n261\t        const alt = node.getAttribute('alt');\n262\t        if (alt) {\n263\t          return `$${alt}$`;\n264\t        }\n265\t        return '';\n266\t      }\n267\t\n268\t      // Handle math elements (KaTeX/MathJax)\n269\t      if (node.classList.contains('katex') || node.classList.contains('math') ||\n270\t          tag === 'mjx-container' || node.classList.contains('MathJax')) {\n271\t        const annotation = node.querySelector('annotation[encoding=\"application/x-tex\"]');\n272\t        if (annotation) {\n273\t          return annotation.textContent;\n274\t        }\n275\t        // Try to get the LaTeX from data attributes\n276\t        const tex = node.getAttribute('data-tex') || node.getAttribute('data-latex');\n277\t        if (tex) return tex;\n278\t        // Fallback to text content cleaned up\n279\t        return node.textContent.trim();\n280\t      }\n281\t\n282\t      // Handle spans (just process children)\n283\t      if (tag === 'span') {\n284\t        return nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n285\t      }\n286\t\n287\t      // Default: process children\n288\t      return nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n289\t    }\n290\t\n291\t    function nodeToMarkdownChildren(node) {\n292\t      let result = '';\n293\t      for (const child of node.childNodes) {\n294\t        result += nodeToMarkdown(child);\n295\t      }\n296\t      return result;\n297\t    }\n298\t\n299\t    // Process all elements in order\n300\t    const elements = [];\n301\t    let figureIndex = 0;\n302\t\n303\t    const processElement = (node) => {\n304\t      if (node.nodeType !== Node.ELEMENT_NODE) return;\n305\t\n306\t      const tag = node.tagName.toLowerCase();\n307\t\n308\t      // Skip certain elements\n309\t      if (['script', 'style', 'noscript'].includes(tag)) return;\n310\t\n311\t      // Handle headings\n312\t      if (['h1', 'h2', 'h3', 'h4', 'h5', 'h6'].includes(tag)) {\n313\t        elements.push({\n314\t          type: 'heading',\n315\t          level: parseInt(tag[1]),\n316\t          content: nodeToMarkdownChildren(node).trim()\n317\t        });\n318\t        return;\n319\t      }\n320\t\n321\t      // Handle paragraphs\n322\t      if (tag === 'p') {\n323\t        const content = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node).trim();\n324\t        if (content) {\n325\t          elements.push({\n326\t            type: 'paragraph',\n327\t            content: content\n328\t          });\n329\t        }\n330\t        return;\n331\t      }\n332\t\n333\t      // Handle code blocks\n334\t      if (tag === 'pre') {\n335\t        const codeEl = node.querySelector('code');\n336\t        // Get innerHTML and convert <br> to newlines, then strip remaining HTML\n337\t        let codeHTML = codeEl ? codeEl.innerHTML : node.innerHTML;\n338\t        // Convert <br> and <br/> to newlines\n339\t        let code = codeHTML.replace(/<br\\s*\\/?>/gi, '\\n');\n340\t        // Strip remaining HTML tags\n341\t        const temp = document.createElement('div');\n342\t        temp.innerHTML = code;\n343\t        code = temp.textContent || temp.innerText;\n344\t        const language = codeEl?.className?.match(/language-(\\w+)/)?.[1] || '';\n345\t        elements.push({\n346\t          type: 'code',\n347\t          language,\n348\t          content: code.trim()\n349\t        });\n350\t        return;\n351\t      }\n352\t\n353\t      // Handle blockquotes\n354\t      if (tag === 'blockquote') {\n355\t        const content = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node).trim();\n356\t\n357\t        // Check if the blockquote contains only a formula (common pattern in articles)\n358\t        // Pattern: starts and ends with $ and mostly contains LaTeX content\n359\t        // Allow for some whitespace around the formula\n360\t        const formulaOnlyMatch = content.match(/^\\s*\\$([^$]+)\\$\\s*$/);\n361\t        if (formulaOnlyMatch) {\n362\t          // This is a formula-only blockquote, preserve as blockquote with block formula\n363\t          // Output will be: > $$formula$$\n364\t          const formula = formulaOnlyMatch[1].trim();\n365\t          elements.push({\n366\t            type: 'blockquote-math',\n367\t            content: formula\n368\t          });\n369\t          return;\n370\t        }\n371\t\n372\t        elements.push({\n373\t          type: 'blockquote',\n374\t          content: content\n375\t        });\n376\t        return;\n377\t      }\n378\t\n379\t      // Handle unordered lists\n380\t      if (tag === 'ul') {\n381\t        const items = Array.from(node.querySelectorAll(':scope > li')).map(li =>\n382\t          nodeToMarkdownChildren(li).trim()\n383\t        );\n384\t        elements.push({\n385\t          type: 'unordered-list',\n386\t          items: items.filter(item => item)\n387\t        });\n388\t        return;\n389\t      }\n390\t\n391\t      // Handle ordered lists\n392\t      if (tag === 'ol') {\n393\t        const items = Array.from(node.querySelectorAll(':scope > li')).map(li =>\n394\t          nodeToMarkdownChildren(li).trim()\n395\t        );\n396\t        elements.push({\n397\t          type: 'ordered-list',\n398\t          items: items.filter(item => item)\n399\t        });\n400\t        return;\n401\t      }\n402\t\n403\t      // Handle figures (images with captions)\n404\t      if (tag === 'figure') {\n405\t        figureIndex++;\n406\t        const img = node.querySelector('img');\n407\t        const figcaption = node.querySelector('figcaption');\n408\t        if (img) {\n409\t          elements.push({\n410\t            type: 'figure',\n411\t            index: figureIndex,\n412\t            src: img.src,\n413\t            alt: img.alt || '',\n414\t            caption: figcaption ? nodeToMarkdownChildren(figcaption).trim() : ''\n415\t          });\n416\t        }\n417\t        return;\n418\t      }\n419\t\n420\t      // Handle standalone images (but not formula images - those are inline)\n421\t      if (tag === 'img' && !node.closest('figure')) {\n422\t        // Check if this is a formula image - handle as block formula if standalone\n423\t        if (node.classList.contains('formula')) {\n424\t          const source = node.getAttribute('source');\n425\t          if (source) {\n426\t            // Standalone formula - treat as block formula\n427\t            elements.push({\n428\t              type: 'math-block',\n429\t              content: source\n430\t            });\n431\t            return;\n432\t          }\n433\t        }\n434\t        // Regular image\n435\t        elements.push({\n436\t          type: 'image',\n437\t          src: node.src,\n438\t          alt: node.alt || ''\n439\t        });\n440\t        return;\n441\t      }\n442\t\n443\t      // Handle horizontal rules\n444\t      if (tag === 'hr') {\n445\t        elements.push({ type: 'hr' });\n446\t        return;\n447\t      }\n448\t\n449\t      // Handle div elements that might contain math blocks\n450\t      if (tag === 'div') {\n451\t        // Check for math block\n452\t        const mathEl = node.querySelector('.katex-display, .math-display, mjx-container[display=\"true\"]');\n453\t        if (mathEl) {\n454\t          const annotation = mathEl.querySelector('annotation[encoding=\"application/x-tex\"]');\n455\t          const tex = annotation ? annotation.textContent :\n456\t                     (mathEl.getAttribute('data-tex') || mathEl.textContent);\n457\t          if (tex) {\n458\t            elements.push({\n459\t              type: 'math-block',\n460\t              content: tex.trim()\n461\t            });\n462\t          }\n463\t          return;\n464\t        }\n465\t\n466\t        // For other divs, process children\n467\t        for (const child of node.children) {\n468\t          processElement(child);\n469\t        }\n470\t        return;\n471\t      }\n472\t\n473\t      // For other block elements, try to process children\n474\t      if (['section', 'article', 'main', 'aside', 'header', 'footer', 'details'].includes(tag)) {\n475\t        for (const child of node.children) {\n476\t          processElement(child);\n477\t        }\n478\t      }\n479\t    };\n480\t\n481\t    for (const child of articleBody.children) {\n482\t      processElement(child);\n483\t    }\n484\t\n485\t    return { title, elements };\n486\t  });\n487\t\n488\t  await browser.close();\n489\t\n490\t  if (content) {\n491\t    content.metadata = metadata;\n492\t  }\n493\t\n494\t  return content;\n495\t}\n496\t\n497\t/**\n498\t * Post-process markdown to fix formatting issues\n499\t */\n500\tfunction postProcessMarkdown(markdown) {\n501\t  let result = markdown;\n502\t\n503\t  // Unicode character normalization to match article.md style\n504\t  // Normalize curly quotes to straight quotes\n505\t  result = result.replace(/['']/g, \"'\");\n506\t  result = result.replace(/[\"\"]/g, '\"');\n507\t\n508\t  // Normalize em-dash and en-dash to regular dash with spaces\n509\t  result = result.replace(/—/g, ' — ');  // Keep em-dash with spaces for readability\n510\t  result = result.replace(/–/g, '-');     // Convert en-dash to hyphen\n511\t\n512\t  // Normalize ellipsis\n513\t  result = result.replace(/…/g, '...');\n514\t\n515\t  // Fix spacing around inline LaTeX formulas\n516\t  // Pattern: word$formula$word should become word $formula$ word\n517\t  // We need to run multiple passes to handle all cases\n518\t\n519\t  // Pass 1: Add space after formula when followed by word character\n520\t  // Match: $formula$word -> $formula$ word\n521\t  result = result.replace(/(\\$[^$\\n]+\\$)([a-zA-Zа-яА-ЯёЁ])/g, (match, formula, nextChar) => {\n522\t    return formula + ' ' + nextChar;\n523\t  });\n524\t\n525\t  // Pass 2: Add space before formula when preceded by word character\n526\t  // Match: word$formula$ -> word $formula$\n527\t  result = result.replace(/([a-zA-Zа-яА-ЯёЁ])(\\$[^$\\n]+\\$)/g, (match, prevChar, formula) => {\n528\t    return prevChar + ' ' + formula;\n529\t  });\n530\t\n531\t  // Fix multiple inline formulas on the same line (common pattern from inline extraction)\n532\t  // Pattern: $formula1$$formula2$ -> $formula1$\\n\\n$formula2$\n533\t  result = result.replace(/(\\$[^$\\n]+\\$)\\$([^$\\n]+\\$)/g, (match, formula1, formula2) => {\n534\t    return formula1 + '\\n\\n$' + formula2;\n535\t  });\n536\t\n537\t  // Note: We removed the \"clean up whitespace inside formulas\" regexes because they\n538\t  // were incorrectly matching and removing spaces BETWEEN formula and adjacent text.\n539\t  // The formulas from the source already have correct internal spacing.\n540\t\n541\t  // Fix double spaces (but not in code blocks)\n542\t  result = result.replace(/([^\\n`])  +/g, (match, char) => {\n543\t    return char + ' ';\n544\t  });\n545\t\n546\t  // Clean up extra spaces around em-dashes that we may have introduced\n547\t  result = result.replace(/\\s+—\\s+/g, ' — ');\n548\t\n549\t  // Fix stray standalone $ signs that might have been left from parsing\n550\t  // Pattern: $\\n\\n$ or just a standalone $ on its own line should be removed\n551\t  result = result.replace(/^\\$\\s*$/gm, '');\n552\t\n553\t  return result;\n554\t}\n555\t\n556\t/**\n557\t * Format metadata as a markdown block to be placed after the title\n558\t */\n559\tfunction formatMetadataBlock(metadata) {\n560\t  if (!metadata) return [];\n561\t\n562\t  const lines = [];\n563\t\n564\t  // Author line\n565\t  if (metadata.author) {\n566\t    const authorName = metadata.authorFullName\n567\t      ? `${metadata.authorFullName} (${metadata.author})`\n568\t      : metadata.author;\n569\t    const authorLink = metadata.authorUrl\n570\t      ? `[${authorName}](${metadata.authorUrl})`\n571\t      : authorName;\n572\t    lines.push(`**Author:** ${authorLink}`);\n573\t  }\n574\t\n575\t  // Original author (for translations)\n576\t  if (metadata.originalAuthor) {\n577\t    lines.push(`**${metadata.originalAuthor}**`);\n578\t  }\n579\t\n580\t  // Publication date\n581\t  if (metadata.publishDate) {\n582\t    const date = new Date(metadata.publishDate);\n583\t    const formatted = date.toLocaleDateString('en-US', {\n584\t      year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric'\n585\t    });\n586\t    let dateLine = `**Published:** ${formatted}`;\n587\t    if (metadata.dateModified) {\n588\t      const modDate = new Date(metadata.dateModified);\n589\t      const modFormatted = modDate.toLocaleDateString('en-US', {\n590\t        year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric'\n591\t      });\n592\t      if (modFormatted !== formatted) {\n593\t        dateLine += ` (updated ${modFormatted})`;\n594\t      }\n595\t    }\n596\t    lines.push(dateLine);\n597\t  }\n598\t\n599\t  // Reading time and difficulty\n600\t  const infoItems = [];\n601\t  if (metadata.readingTime) infoItems.push(`Reading time: ${metadata.readingTime}`);\n602\t  if (metadata.difficulty) infoItems.push(`Difficulty: ${metadata.difficulty}`);\n603\t  if (metadata.views) infoItems.push(`Views: ${metadata.views}`);\n604\t  if (infoItems.length > 0) {\n605\t    lines.push(`**${infoItems.join(' | ')}**`);\n606\t  }\n607\t\n608\t  // Hubs\n609\t  if (metadata.hubs && metadata.hubs.length > 0) {\n610\t    lines.push(`**Hubs:** ${metadata.hubs.join(', ')}`);\n611\t  }\n612\t\n613\t  // Tags\n614\t  if (metadata.tags && metadata.tags.length > 0) {\n615\t    lines.push(`**Tags:** ${metadata.tags.join(', ')}`);\n616\t  }\n617\t\n618\t  return lines;\n619\t}\n620\t\n621\t/**\n622\t * Convert extracted content to markdown\n623\t */\n624\tfunction contentToMarkdown(content, article) {\n625\t  if (!content) return '';\n626\t\n627\t  const lines = [];\n628\t\n629\t  // Add title\n630\t  if (content.title) {\n631\t    lines.push(`# ${content.title}`);\n632\t    lines.push('');\n633\t  }\n634\t\n635\t  // Add metadata block after title\n636\t  const metadataLines = formatMetadataBlock(content.metadata);\n637\t  if (metadataLines.length > 0) {\n638\t    for (const line of metadataLines) {\n639\t      lines.push(line);\n640\t    }\n641\t    lines.push('');\n642\t    lines.push('---');\n643\t    lines.push('');\n644\t  }\n645\t\n646\t  let imageIndex = 1;\n647\t\n648\t  for (const element of content.elements) {\n649\t    switch (element.type) {\n650\t      case 'heading':\n651\t        const prefix = '#'.repeat(element.level);\n652\t        lines.push(`${prefix} ${element.content}`);\n653\t        lines.push('');\n654\t        break;\n655\t\n656\t      case 'paragraph':\n657\t        if (element.content) {\n658\t          lines.push(element.content);\n659\t          lines.push('');\n660\t        }\n661\t        break;\n662\t\n663\t      case 'code':\n664\t        lines.push('```' + (element.language || ''));\n665\t        lines.push(element.content);\n666\t        lines.push('```');\n667\t        lines.push('');\n668\t        break;\n669\t\n670\t      case 'blockquote':\n671\t        const quoteLines = element.content.split('\\n');\n672\t        for (const line of quoteLines) {\n673\t          lines.push(`> ${line}`);\n674\t        }\n675\t        lines.push('');\n676\t        break;\n677\t\n678\t      case 'unordered-list':\n679\t        for (const item of element.items) {\n680\t          // Handle multi-line list items\n681\t          const itemLines = item.split('\\n');\n682\t          lines.push(`- ${itemLines[0]}`);\n683\t          for (let i = 1; i < itemLines.length; i++) {\n684\t            lines.push(`  ${itemLines[i]}`);\n685\t          }\n686\t        }\n687\t        lines.push('');\n688\t        break;\n689\t\n690\t      case 'ordered-list':\n691\t        element.items.forEach((item, i) => {\n692\t          const itemLines = item.split('\\n');\n693\t          lines.push(`${i + 1}. ${itemLines[0]}`);\n694\t          for (let j = 1; j < itemLines.length; j++) {\n695\t            lines.push(`   ${itemLines[j]}`);\n696\t          }\n697\t        });\n698\t        lines.push('');\n699\t        break;\n700\t\n701\t      case 'figure':\n702\t        // Use local image path with figure number\n703\t        const figureMatch = element.caption.match(/(?:Figure|Рис\\.?|Рисунок)\\s*(\\d+)/i);\n704\t        const figNum = figureMatch ? figureMatch[1] : element.index;\n705\t        const ext = element.src.includes('.jpeg') || element.src.includes('.jpg') ? 'jpg' : 'png';\n706\t\n707\t        // Simple alt text format: \"Figure N\" only, not the full caption\n708\t        // This matches the article.md format\n709\t        const simpleAltText = `Figure ${figNum}`;\n710\t        lines.push(`![${simpleAltText}](images/figure-${figNum}.${ext})`);\n711\t        if (element.caption) {\n712\t          // Caption already includes bold formatting from extraction (e.g., **Figure 1.** ...)\n713\t          // Don't wrap it again in bold, just output as-is\n714\t          lines.push('');\n715\t          lines.push(element.caption);\n716\t        }\n717\t        lines.push('');\n718\t        break;\n719\t\n720\t      case 'image':\n721\t        // Use local path format\n722\t        const imgExt = element.src.includes('.jpeg') || element.src.includes('.jpg') ? 'jpg' : 'png';\n723\t        lines.push(`![${element.alt}](images/image-${String(imageIndex).padStart(2, '0')}.${imgExt})`);\n724\t        lines.push('');\n725\t        imageIndex++;\n726\t        break;\n727\t\n728\t      case 'math-block':\n729\t        lines.push('$$' + element.content + '$$');\n730\t        lines.push('');\n731\t        break;\n732\t\n733\t      case 'blockquote-math':\n734\t        // Formula in a blockquote - preserve blockquote formatting with block formula\n735\t        lines.push('> $$' + element.content + '$$');\n736\t        lines.push('');\n737\t        break;\n738\t\n739\t      case 'hr':\n740\t        lines.push('---');\n741\t        lines.push('');\n742\t        break;\n743\t    }\n744\t  }\n745\t\n746\t  const rawMarkdown = lines.join('\\n').trim() + '\\n';\n747\t  return postProcessMarkdown(rawMarkdown);\n748\t}\n749\t\n750\t/**\n751\t * Download article and save as markdown\n752\t */\n753\tasync function downloadArticle(article, options) {\n754\t  const archivePath = join(ROOT_DIR, article.archivePath);\n755\t  const outputFileName = options.outputFile || article.markdownFile;\n756\t  const markdownPath = join(archivePath, outputFileName);\n757\t\n758\t  console.log(`\\n📥 Downloading ${article.title} (${article.version})`);\n759\t  console.log('='.repeat(70));\n760\t  console.log(`   URL: ${article.url}`);\n761\t  console.log(`   Target: ${markdownPath}`);\n762\t\n763\t  // Ensure archive directory exists\n764\t  if (!existsSync(archivePath)) {\n765\t    mkdirSync(archivePath, { recursive: true });\n766\t    console.log(`   Created directory: ${archivePath}`);\n767\t  }\n768\t\n769\t  // Extract content from web page\n770\t  console.log('   Extracting content from web page...');\n771\t  const content = await extractArticleContent(article, options.verbose);\n772\t\n773\t  if (!content) {\n774\t    console.error('   ❌ Failed to extract article content');\n775\t    return { success: false, error: 'Failed to extract content' };\n776\t  }\n777\t\n778\t  console.log(`   ✅ Extracted ${content.elements.length} elements`);\n779\t\n780\t  // Convert to markdown\n781\t  console.log('   Converting to markdown...');\n782\t  const markdown = contentToMarkdown(content, article);\n783\t\n784\t  if (options.dryRun) {\n785\t    console.log('   [DRY RUN] Would save markdown file');\n786\t    console.log(`   Preview (first 500 chars):\\n${markdown.substring(0, 500)}...`);\n787\t    return { success: true, dryRun: true };\n788\t  }\n789\t\n790\t  // Save markdown file\n791\t  writeFileSync(markdownPath, markdown, 'utf-8');\n792\t  console.log(`   ✅ Saved ${markdownPath}`);\n793\t  console.log(`   File size: ${(markdown.length / 1024).toFixed(1)} KB`);\n794\t\n795\t  return { success: true, path: markdownPath, size: markdown.length };\n796\t}\n797\t\n798\t/**\n799\t * Main function\n800\t */\n801\tasync function main() {\n802\t  const options = parseArgs();\n803\t\n804\t  // Show help if no version specified\n805\t  if (!options.all && !options.version) {\n806\t    console.log(`\n807\tUsage: node scripts/download-article.mjs [version] [options]\n808\t\n809\tOptions:\n810\t  --all         Download all articles\n811\t  --downloaded  Save as downloaded.md instead of article.md\n812\t  --dry-run     Show what would be done without making changes\n813\t  --verbose     Show detailed output\n814\t\n815\tExamples:\n816\t  node scripts/download-article.mjs 0.0.1\n817\t  node scripts/download-article.mjs --all\n818\t  node scripts/download-article.mjs --all --downloaded\n819\t  node scripts/download-article.mjs 0.0.2 --dry-run\n820\t`);\n821\t    process.exit(0);\n822\t  }\n823\t\n824\t  // Get articles to process\n825\t  let articles = [];\n826\t  if (options.all) {\n827\t    articles = getAllArticles();\n828\t  } else if (options.version) {\n829\t    articles = [getArticle(options.version)];\n830\t  }\n831\t\n832\t  console.log('🚀 Article Download Script');\n833\t  console.log('==========================');\n834\t  if (options.dryRun) {\n835\t    console.log('⚠️  DRY RUN MODE - No files will be created\\n');\n836\t  }\n837\t\n838\t  const results = [];\n839\t\n840\t  for (const article of articles) {\n841\t    try {\n842\t      const result = await downloadArticle(article, options);\n843\t      results.push({ article, ...result });\n844\t    } catch (error) {\n845\t      console.error(`\\n❌ Error downloading ${article.version}:`, error.message);\n846\t      results.push({ article, success: false, error: error.message });\n847\t    }\n848\t  }\n849\t\n850\t  // Print summary\n851\t  console.log('\\n' + '='.repeat(70));\n852\t  console.log('📊 DOWNLOAD SUMMARY');\n853\t  console.log('='.repeat(70));\n854\t\n855\t  for (const result of results) {\n856\t    const status = result.success ? '✅' : '❌';\n857\t    const details = result.success\n858\t      ? (result.dryRun ? 'dry run' : `${(result.size / 1024).toFixed(1)} KB`)\n859\t      : result.error;\n860\t    console.log(`   ${status} ${result.article.version}: ${details}`);\n861\t  }\n862\t\n863\t  console.log('\\n' + '='.repeat(70));\n864\t  process.exit(results.every(r => r.success) ? 0 : 1);\n865\t}\n866\t\n867\tmain().catch(error => {\n868\t  console.error('❌ Error:', error);\n869\t  process.exit(1);\n870\t});\n871\t"

      "content": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n\n/**\n * Script to download article content from web pages and convert to markdown\n *\n * This script extracts article content from Habr and converts it to markdown format.\n * It handles:\n * - Title extraction\n * - Headings (h2, h3, h4)\n * - Paragraphs with inline links\n * - Code blocks with syntax highlighting (preserves newlines from <br> tags)\n * - Lists (ordered and unordered)\n * - Blockquotes\n * - Images and figures with captions\n * - Links preservation\n * - LaTeX math formulas (extracted from img.formula elements' `source` attribute)\n *\n * FORMULA EXTRACTION:\n * Habr renders formulas as SVG/PNG images with class \"formula\". The original LaTeX\n * source code is stored in the `source` attribute of these img elements. This script\n * automatically extracts and converts them to proper LaTeX markdown format ($...$).\n *\n * Usage:\n *   node scripts/download-article.mjs [version]\n *   node scripts/download-article.mjs --all\n *\n * Examples:\n *   node scripts/download-article.mjs 0.0.1\n *   node scripts/download-article.mjs --all\n */\n\nimport { chromium } from 'playwright';\nimport { writeFileSync, mkdirSync, existsSync } from 'fs';\nimport { fileURLToPath } from 'url';\nimport { dirname, join } from 'path';\nimport { getArticle, getAllArticles } from './articles-config.mjs';\n\nconst __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);\nconst __dirname = dirname(__filename);\nconst ROOT_DIR = join(__dirname, '..');\n\n/**\n * Parse command line arguments\n */\nfunction parseArgs() {\n  const args = process.argv.slice(2);\n  const options = {\n    version: null,\n    all: false,\n    dryRun: false,\n    verbose: false,\n    outputFile: 'article.md'  // Default output file, can be changed with --downloaded\n  };\n\n  for (const arg of args) {\n    if (arg === '--all') {\n      options.all = true;\n    } else if (arg === '--dry-run') {\n      options.dryRun = true;\n    } else if (arg === '--verbose' || arg === '-v') {\n      options.verbose = true;\n    } else if (arg === '--downloaded') {\n      options.outputFile = 'downloaded.md';\n    } else if (!arg.startsWith('-')) {\n      options.version = arg;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return options;\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract article content from web page and convert to markdown\n */\nasync function extractArticleContent(article, verbose = false) {\n  if (verbose) console.log('   Loading web page:', article.url);\n\n  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });\n  const context = await browser.newContext({\n    viewport: { width: 1920, height: 1080 },\n    userAgent: 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36'\n  });\n  const page = await context.newPage();\n\n  // Navigate to the page\n  await page.goto(article.url, {\n    waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded',\n    timeout: 120000\n  });\n\n  // Wait for article body to appear\n  await page.waitForSelector('.article-formatted-body', { timeout: 30000 });\n\n  // Scroll through the page to trigger lazy loading\n  await page.evaluate(async () => {\n    const scrollHeight = document.documentElement.scrollHeight;\n    const viewportHeight = window.innerHeight;\n    const scrollSteps = Math.ceil(scrollHeight / viewportHeight);\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < scrollSteps; i++) {\n      window.scrollTo(0, i * viewportHeight);\n      await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));\n    }\n    window.scrollTo(0, 0);\n  });\n\n  // Wait for dynamic content\n  await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n\n  // Extract article metadata from the page header area\n  const metadata = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const meta = {};\n\n    // Author\n    const authorEl = document.querySelector('.tm-user-info__username');\n    if (authorEl) {\n      meta.author = authorEl.innerText.trim();\n      meta.authorUrl = authorEl.href || null;\n    }\n\n    // Publication date\n    const timeEl = document.querySelector('time[datetime]');\n    if (timeEl) {\n      meta.publishDate = timeEl.getAttribute('datetime');\n      meta.publishDateText = timeEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // Reading time\n    const readTimeEl = document.querySelector('.tm-article-reading-time__label');\n    if (readTimeEl) {\n      meta.readingTime = readTimeEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // Difficulty level\n    const diffEl = document.querySelector('.tm-article-complexity__label');\n    if (diffEl) {\n      meta.difficulty = diffEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // Views\n    const viewsEl = document.querySelector('.tm-icon-counter__value');\n    if (viewsEl) {\n      meta.views = viewsEl.getAttribute('title') || viewsEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // Hubs (use specific hub link selector to avoid duplicates)\n    const hubEls = document.querySelectorAll('.tm-publication-hub__link');\n    meta.hubs = Array.from(hubEls).map(el => {\n      // Get only the first span text (hub name), not the asterisk\n      const nameSpan = el.querySelector('span:first-child');\n      return nameSpan ? nameSpan.innerText.trim() : el.innerText.trim().replace(/\\s*\\*\\s*$/, '');\n    });\n\n    // Tags from meta keywords\n    const keywordsMeta = document.querySelector('meta[name=\"keywords\"]');\n    if (keywordsMeta) {\n      const content = keywordsMeta.getAttribute('content');\n      if (content) {\n        meta.tags = content.split(',').map(t => t.trim()).filter(Boolean);\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Translation / original author info\n    const originEl = document.querySelector('.tm-article-presenter__origin');\n    if (originEl) {\n      meta.originalAuthor = originEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // LD+JSON structured data for additional metadata\n    const ldJsonScript = document.querySelector('script[type=\"application/ld+json\"]');\n    if (ldJsonScript) {\n      try {\n        const ldData = JSON.parse(ldJsonScript.textContent);\n        if (ldData.dateModified) meta.dateModified = ldData.dateModified;\n        if (ldData.author?.name) meta.authorFullName = ldData.author.name;\n      } catch (e) {\n        // ignore parse errors\n      }\n    }\n\n    return meta;\n  });\n\n  if (verbose) {\n    console.log('   Metadata extracted:', JSON.stringify(metadata, null, 2));\n  }\n\n  // Extract article content as structured data with HTML processing\n  const content = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const articleBody = document.querySelector('.article-formatted-body');\n    if (!articleBody) return null;\n\n    // Get article title\n    const titleEl = document.querySelector('article h1');\n    const title = titleEl ? titleEl.innerText.trim() : '';\n\n    /**\n     * Convert an HTML element to markdown, preserving links and formatting\n     */\n    function nodeToMarkdown(node, context = {}) {\n      if (node.nodeType === Node.TEXT_NODE) {\n        return node.textContent;\n      }\n\n      if (node.nodeType !== Node.ELEMENT_NODE) return '';\n\n      const tag = node.tagName.toLowerCase();\n\n      // Skip script, style, etc.\n      if (['script', 'style', 'noscript', 'svg'].includes(tag)) return '';\n\n      // Handle links\n      if (tag === 'a') {\n        const href = node.getAttribute('href');\n        const text = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n        if (href && text) {\n          return `[${text}](${href})`;\n        }\n        return text;\n      }\n\n      // Handle bold\n      if (tag === 'strong' || tag === 'b') {\n        const text = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n        return text ? `**${text}**` : '';\n      }\n\n      // Handle italic\n      if (tag === 'em' || tag === 'i') {\n        const text = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n        return text ? `*${text}*` : '';\n      }\n\n      // Handle inline code\n      if (tag === 'code' && !node.closest('pre')) {\n        const text = node.textContent;\n        return text ? `\\`${text}\\`` : '';\n      }\n\n      // Handle line breaks\n      if (tag === 'br') {\n        return '\\n';\n      }\n\n      // Handle subscript/superscript (common in math)\n      if (tag === 'sub') {\n        return `₍${node.textContent}₎`;\n      }\n      if (tag === 'sup') {\n        return `^${node.textContent}`;\n      }\n\n      // Handle formula images (Habr stores LaTeX in `source` attribute)\n      if (tag === 'img' && node.classList.contains('formula')) {\n        const source = node.getAttribute('source');\n        if (source) {\n          // Inline formula - wrap in single $\n          return `$${source}$`;\n        }\n        // Fallback to alt text\n        const alt = node.getAttribute('alt');\n        if (alt) {\n          return `$${alt}$`;\n        }\n        return '';\n      }\n\n      // Handle math elements (KaTeX/MathJax)\n      if (node.classList.contains('katex') || node.classList.contains('math') ||\n          tag === 'mjx-container' || node.classList.contains('MathJax')) {\n        const annotation = node.querySelector('annotation[encoding=\"application/x-tex\"]');\n        if (annotation) {\n          return annotation.textContent;\n        }\n        // Try to get the LaTeX from data attributes\n        const tex = node.getAttribute('data-tex') || node.getAttribute('data-latex');\n        if (tex) return tex;\n        // Fallback to text content cleaned up\n        return node.textContent.trim();\n      }\n\n      // Handle spans (just process children)\n      if (tag === 'span') {\n        return nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n      }\n\n      // Default: process children\n      return nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n    }\n\n    function nodeToMarkdownChildren(node) {\n      let result = '';\n      for (const child of node.childNodes) {\n        result += nodeToMarkdown(child);\n      }\n      return result;\n    }\n\n    // Process all elements in order\n    const elements = [];\n    let figureIndex = 0;\n\n    const processElement = (node) => {\n      if (node.nodeType !== Node.ELEMENT_NODE) return;\n\n      const tag = node.tagName.toLowerCase();\n\n      // Skip certain elements\n      if (['script', 'style', 'noscript'].includes(tag)) return;\n\n      // Handle headings\n      if (['h1', 'h2', 'h3', 'h4', 'h5', 'h6'].includes(tag)) {\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'heading',\n          level: parseInt(tag[1]),\n          content: nodeToMarkdownChildren(node).trim()\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle paragraphs\n      if (tag === 'p') {\n        const content = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node).trim();\n        if (content) {\n          elements.push({\n            type: 'paragraph',\n            content: content\n          });\n        }\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle code blocks\n      if (tag === 'pre') {\n        const codeEl = node.querySelector('code');\n        // Get innerHTML and convert <br> to newlines, then strip remaining HTML\n        let codeHTML = codeEl ? codeEl.innerHTML : node.innerHTML;\n        // Convert <br> and <br/> to newlines\n        let code = codeHTML.replace(/<br\\s*\\/?>/gi, '\\n');\n        // Strip remaining HTML tags\n        const temp = document.createElement('div');\n        temp.innerHTML = code;\n        code = temp.textContent || temp.innerText;\n        const language = codeEl?.className?.match(/language-(\\w+)/)?.[1] || '';\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'code',\n          language,\n          content: code.trim()\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle blockquotes\n      if (tag === 'blockquote') {\n        const content = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node).trim();\n\n        // Check if the blockquote contains only a formula (common pattern in articles)\n        // Pattern: starts and ends with $ and mostly contains LaTeX content\n        // Allow for some whitespace around the formula\n        const formulaOnlyMatch = content.match(/^\\s*\\$([^$]+)\\$\\s*$/);\n        if (formulaOnlyMatch) {\n          // This is a formula-only blockquote, preserve as blockquote with block formula\n          // Output will be: > $$formula$$\n          const formula = formulaOnlyMatch[1].trim();\n          elements.push({\n            type: 'blockquote-math',\n            content: formula\n          });\n          return;\n        }\n\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'blockquote',\n          content: content\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle unordered lists\n      if (tag === 'ul') {\n        const items = Array.from(node.querySelectorAll(':scope > li')).map(li =>\n          nodeToMarkdownChildren(li).trim()\n        );\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'unordered-list',\n          items: items.filter(item => item)\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle ordered lists\n      if (tag === 'ol') {\n        const items = Array.from(node.querySelectorAll(':scope > li')).map(li =>\n          nodeToMarkdownChildren(li).trim()\n        );\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'ordered-list',\n          items: items.filter(item => item)\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle figures (images with captions)\n      if (tag === 'figure') {\n        figureIndex++;\n        const img = node.querySelector('img');\n        const figcaption = node.querySelector('figcaption');\n        if (img) {\n          elements.push({\n            type: 'figure',\n            index: figureIndex,\n            src: img.src,\n            alt: img.alt || '',\n            caption: figcaption ? nodeToMarkdownChildren(figcaption).trim() : ''\n          });\n        }\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle standalone images (but not formula images - those are inline)\n      if (tag === 'img' && !node.closest('figure')) {\n        // Check if this is a formula image - handle as block formula if standalone\n        if (node.classList.contains('formula')) {\n          const source = node.getAttribute('source');\n          if (source) {\n            // Standalone formula - treat as block formula\n            elements.push({\n              type: 'math-block',\n              content: source\n            });\n            return;\n          }\n        }\n        // Regular image\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'image',\n          src: node.src,\n          alt: node.alt || ''\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle horizontal rules\n      if (tag === 'hr') {\n        elements.push({ type: 'hr' });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle div elements that might contain math blocks\n      if (tag === 'div') {\n        // Check for math block\n        const mathEl = node.querySelector('.katex-display, .math-display, mjx-container[display=\"true\"]');\n        if (mathEl) {\n          const annotation = mathEl.querySelector('annotation[encoding=\"application/x-tex\"]');\n          const tex = annotation ? annotation.textContent :\n                     (mathEl.getAttribute('data-tex') || mathEl.textContent);\n          if (tex) {\n            elements.push({\n              type: 'math-block',\n              content: tex.trim()\n            });\n          }\n          return;\n        }\n\n        // For other divs, process children\n        for (const child of node.children) {\n          processElement(child);\n        }\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // For other block elements, try to process children\n      if (['section', 'article', 'main', 'aside', 'header', 'footer', 'details'].includes(tag)) {\n        for (const child of node.children) {\n          processElement(child);\n        }\n      }\n    };\n\n    for (const child of articleBody.children) {\n      processElement(child);\n    }\n\n    return { title, elements };\n  });\n\n  await browser.close();\n\n  if (content) {\n    content.metadata = metadata;\n  }\n\n  return content;\n}\n\n/**\n * Post-process markdown to fix formatting issues\n */\nfunction postProcessMarkdown(markdown) {\n  let result = markdown;\n\n  // Unicode character normalization to match article.md style\n  // Normalize curly quotes to straight quotes\n  result = result.replace(/['']/g, \"'\");\n  result = result.replace(/[\"\"]/g, '\"');\n\n  // Normalize em-dash and en-dash to regular dash with spaces\n  result = result.replace(/—/g, ' — ');  // Keep em-dash with spaces for readability\n  result = result.replace(/–/g, '-');     // Convert en-dash to hyphen\n\n  // Normalize ellipsis\n  result = result.replace(/…/g, '...');\n\n  // Fix spacing around inline LaTeX formulas\n  // Pattern: word$formula$word should become word $formula$ word\n  // We need to run multiple passes to handle all cases\n\n  // Pass 1: Add space after formula when followed by word character\n  // Match: $formula$word -> $formula$ word\n  result = result.replace(/(\\$[^$\\n]+\\$)([a-zA-Zа-яА-ЯёЁ])/g, (match, formula, nextChar) => {\n    return formula + ' ' + nextChar;\n  });\n\n  // Pass 2: Add space before formula when preceded by word character\n  // Match: word$formula$ -> word $formula$\n  result = result.replace(/([a-zA-Zа-яА-ЯёЁ])(\\$[^$\\n]+\\$)/g, (match, prevChar, formula) => {\n    return prevChar + ' ' + formula;\n  });\n\n  // Fix multiple inline formulas on the same line (common pattern from inline extraction)\n  // Pattern: $formula1$$formula2$ -> $formula1$\\n\\n$formula2$\n  result = result.replace(/(\\$[^$\\n]+\\$)\\$([^$\\n]+\\$)/g, (match, formula1, formula2) => {\n    return formula1 + '\\n\\n$' + formula2;\n  });\n\n  // Note: We removed the \"clean up whitespace inside formulas\" regexes because they\n  // were incorrectly matching and removing spaces BETWEEN formula and adjacent text.\n  // The formulas from the source already have correct internal spacing.\n\n  // Fix double spaces (but not in code blocks)\n  result = result.replace(/([^\\n`])  +/g, (match, char) => {\n    return char + ' ';\n  });\n\n  // Clean up extra spaces around em-dashes that we may have introduced\n  result = result.replace(/\\s+—\\s+/g, ' — ');\n\n  // Fix stray standalone $ signs that might have been left from parsing\n  // Pattern: $\\n\\n$ or just a standalone $ on its own line should be removed\n  result = result.replace(/^\\$\\s*$/gm, '');\n\n  return result;\n}\n\n/**\n * Format metadata as a markdown block to be placed after the title\n */\nfunction formatMetadataBlock(metadata) {\n  if (!metadata) return [];\n\n  const lines = [];\n\n  // Author line\n  if (metadata.author) {\n    const authorName = metadata.authorFullName\n      ? `${metadata.authorFullName} (${metadata.author})`\n      : metadata.author;\n    const authorLink = metadata.authorUrl\n      ? `[${authorName}](${metadata.authorUrl})`\n      : authorName;\n    lines.push(`**Author:** ${authorLink}`);\n  }\n\n  // Original author (for translations)\n  if (metadata.originalAuthor) {\n    lines.push(`**${metadata.originalAuthor}**`);\n  }\n\n  // Publication date\n  if (metadata.publishDate) {\n    const date = new Date(metadata.publishDate);\n    const formatted = date.toLocaleDateString('en-US', {\n      year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric'\n    });\n    let dateLine = `**Published:** ${formatted}`;\n    if (metadata.dateModified) {\n      const modDate = new Date(metadata.dateModified);\n      const modFormatted = modDate.toLocaleDateString('en-US', {\n        year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric'\n      });\n      if (modFormatted !== formatted) {\n        dateLine += ` (updated ${modFormatted})`;\n      }\n    }\n    lines.push(dateLine);\n  }\n\n  // Reading time and difficulty\n  const infoItems = [];\n  if (metadata.readingTime) infoItems.push(`Reading time: ${metadata.readingTime}`);\n  if (metadata.difficulty) infoItems.push(`Difficulty: ${metadata.difficulty}`);\n  if (metadata.views) infoItems.push(`Views: ${metadata.views}`);\n  if (infoItems.length > 0) {\n    lines.push(`**${infoItems.join(' | ')}**`);\n  }\n\n  // Hubs\n  if (metadata.hubs && metadata.hubs.length > 0) {\n    lines.push(`**Hubs:** ${metadata.hubs.join(', ')}`);\n  }\n\n  // Tags\n  if (metadata.tags && metadata.tags.length > 0) {\n    lines.push(`**Tags:** ${metadata.tags.join(', ')}`);\n  }\n\n  return lines;\n}\n\n/**\n * Convert extracted content to markdown\n */\nfunction contentToMarkdown(content, article) {\n  if (!content) return '';\n\n  const lines = [];\n\n  // Add title\n  if (content.title) {\n    lines.push(`# ${content.title}`);\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n\n  // Add metadata block after title\n  const metadataLines = formatMetadataBlock(content.metadata);\n  if (metadataLines.length > 0) {\n    for (const line of metadataLines) {\n      lines.push(line);\n    }\n    lines.push('');\n    lines.push('---');\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n\n  let imageIndex = 1;\n\n  for (const element of content.elements) {\n    switch (element.type) {\n      case 'heading':\n        const prefix = '#'.repeat(element.level);\n        lines.push(`${prefix} ${element.content}`);\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'paragraph':\n        if (element.content) {\n          lines.push(element.content);\n          lines.push('');\n        }\n        break;\n\n      case 'code':\n        lines.push('```' + (element.language || ''));\n        lines.push(element.content);\n        lines.push('```');\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'blockquote':\n        const quoteLines = element.content.split('\\n');\n        for (const line of quoteLines) {\n          lines.push(`> ${line}`);\n        }\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'unordered-list':\n        for (const item of element.items) {\n          // Handle multi-line list items\n          const itemLines = item.split('\\n');\n          lines.push(`- ${itemLines[0]}`);\n          for (let i = 1; i < itemLines.length; i++) {\n            lines.push(`  ${itemLines[i]}`);\n          }\n        }\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'ordered-list':\n        element.items.forEach((item, i) => {\n          const itemLines = item.split('\\n');\n          lines.push(`${i + 1}. ${itemLines[0]}`);\n          for (let j = 1; j < itemLines.length; j++) {\n            lines.push(`   ${itemLines[j]}`);\n          }\n        });\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'figure':\n        // Use local image path with figure number\n        const figureMatch = element.caption.match(/(?:Figure|Рис\\.?|Рисунок)\\s*(\\d+)/i);\n        const figNum = figureMatch ? figureMatch[1] : element.index;\n        const ext = element.src.includes('.jpeg') || element.src.includes('.jpg') ? 'jpg' : 'png';\n\n        // Simple alt text format: \"Figure N\" only, not the full caption\n        // This matches the article.md format\n        const simpleAltText = `Figure ${figNum}`;\n        lines.push(`![${simpleAltText}](images/figure-${figNum}.${ext})`);\n        if (element.caption) {\n          // Caption already includes bold formatting from extraction (e.g., **Figure 1.** ...)\n          // Don't wrap it again in bold, just output as-is\n          lines.push('');\n          lines.push(element.caption);\n        }\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'image':\n        // Use local path format\n        const imgExt = element.src.includes('.jpeg') || element.src.includes('.jpg') ? 'jpg' : 'png';\n        lines.push(`![${element.alt}](images/image-${String(imageIndex).padStart(2, '0')}.${imgExt})`);\n        lines.push('');\n        imageIndex++;\n        break;\n\n      case 'math-block':\n        lines.push('$$' + element.content + '$$');\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'blockquote-math':\n        // Formula in a blockquote - preserve blockquote formatting with block formula\n        lines.push('> $$' + element.content + '$$');\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'hr':\n        lines.push('---');\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n    }\n  }\n\n  const rawMarkdown = lines.join('\\n').trim() + '\\n';\n  return postProcessMarkdown(rawMarkdown);\n}\n\n/**\n * Download article and save as markdown\n */\nasync function downloadArticle(article, options) {\n  const archivePath = join(ROOT_DIR, article.archivePath);\n  const outputFileName = options.outputFile || article.markdownFile;\n  const markdownPath = join(archivePath, outputFileName);\n\n  console.log(`\\n📥 Downloading ${article.title} (${article.version})`);\n  console.log('='.repeat(70));\n  console.log(`   URL: ${article.url}`);\n  console.log(`   Target: ${markdownPath}`);\n\n  // Ensure archive directory exists\n  if (!existsSync(archivePath)) {\n    mkdirSync(archivePath, { recursive: true });\n    console.log(`   Created directory: ${archivePath}`);\n  }\n\n  // Extract content from web page\n  console.log('   Extracting content from web page...');\n  const content = await extractArticleContent(article, options.verbose);\n\n  if (!content) {\n    console.error('   ❌ Failed to extract article content');\n    return { success: false, error: 'Failed to extract content' };\n  }\n\n  console.log(`   ✅ Extracted ${content.elements.length} elements`);\n\n  // Convert to markdown\n  console.log('   Converting to markdown...');\n  const markdown = contentToMarkdown(content, article);\n\n  if (options.dryRun) {\n    console.log('   [DRY RUN] Would save markdown file');\n    console.log(`   Preview (first 500 chars):\\n${markdown.substring(0, 500)}...`);\n    return { success: true, dryRun: true };\n  }\n\n  // Save markdown file\n  writeFileSync(markdownPath, markdown, 'utf-8');\n  console.log(`   ✅ Saved ${markdownPath}`);\n  console.log(`   File size: ${(markdown.length / 1024).toFixed(1)} KB`);\n\n  return { success: true, path: markdownPath, size: markdown.length };\n}\n\n/**\n * Main function\n */\nasync function main() {\n  const options = parseArgs();\n\n  // Show help if no version specified\n  if (!options.all && !options.version) {\n    console.log(`\nUsage: node scripts/download-article.mjs [version] [options]\n\nOptions:\n  --all         Download all articles\n  --downloaded  Save as downloaded.md instead of article.md\n  --dry-run     Show what would be done without making changes\n  --verbose     Show detailed output\n\nExamples:\n  node scripts/download-article.mjs 0.0.1\n  node scripts/download-article.mjs --all\n  node scripts/download-article.mjs --all --downloaded\n  node scripts/download-article.mjs 0.0.2 --dry-run\n`);\n    process.exit(0);\n  }\n\n  // Get articles to process\n  let articles = [];\n  if (options.all) {\n    articles = getAllArticles();\n  } else if (options.version) {\n    articles = [getArticle(options.version)];\n  }\n\n  console.log('🚀 Article Download Script');\n  console.log('==========================');\n  if (options.dryRun) {\n    console.log('⚠️  DRY RUN MODE - No files will be created\\n');\n  }\n\n  const results = [];\n\n  for (const article of articles) {\n    try {\n      const result = await downloadArticle(article, options);\n      results.push({ article, ...result });\n    } catch (error) {\n      console.error(`\\n❌ Error downloading ${article.version}:`, error.message);\n      results.push({ article, success: false, error: error.message });\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Print summary\n  console.log('\\n' + '='.repeat(70));\n  console.log('📊 DOWNLOAD SUMMARY');\n  console.log('='.repeat(70));\n\n  for (const result of results) {\n    const status = result.success ? '✅' : '❌';\n    const details = result.success\n      ? (result.dryRun ? 'dry run' : `${(result.size / 1024).toFixed(1)} KB`)\n      : result.error;\n    console.log(`   ${status} ${result.article.version}: ${details}`);\n  }\n\n  console.log('\\n' + '='.repeat(70));\n  process.exit(results.every(r => r.success) ? 0 : 1);\n}\n\nmain().catch(error => {\n  console.error('❌ Error:', error);\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n",
