fileURLToPath } from 'url';\nimport { dirname, join } from 'path';\nimport https from 'https';\nimport http from 'http';\nimport fs from 'fs';\nimport { getArticle, getAllArticles } from './articles-config.mjs';\n\nconst __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);\nconst __dirname = dirname(__filename);\nconst ROOT_DIR = join(__dirname, '..');\n\n/**\n * Parse command line arguments\n */\nfunction parseArgs() {\n  const args = process.argv.slice(2);\n  const options = {\n    version: null,\n    all: false,\n    images: false,\n    screenshot: false\n  };\n\n  for (const arg of args) {\n    if (arg === '--all') {\n      options.all = true;\n    } else if (arg === '--images') {\n      options.images = true;\n    } else if (arg === '--screenshot') {\n      options.screenshot = true;\n    } else if (!arg.startsWith('-')) {\n      options.version = arg;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return options;\n}\n\n/**\n * Download a file from URL\n */\nfunction downloadFile(url, filepath) {\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n    const protocol = url.startsWith('https') ? https : http;\n    const file = fs.createWriteStream(filepath);\n\n    protocol.get(url, (response) => {\n      // Handle redirects\n      if (response.statusCode === 301 || response.statusCode === 302) {\n        downloadFile(response.headers.location, filepath)\n          .then(resolve)\n          .catch(reject);\n        return;\n      }\n\n      if (response.statusCode !== 200) {\n        reject(new Error(`Failed to download: ${response.statusCode}`));\n        return;\n      }\n\n      response.pipe(file);\n      file.on('finish', () => {\n        file.close();\n        resolve();\n      });\n    }).on('error', (err) => {\n      fs.unlink(filepath, () => {}); // Delete incomplete file\n      reject(err);\n    });\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Download figure images from an article\n */\nasync function downloadImages(article) {\n  const archivePath = join(ROOT_DIR, article.archivePath);\n  const imagesDir = join(archivePath, article.imagesDir);\n\n  // Ensure images directory exists\n  if (!existsSync(imagesDir)) {\n    mkdirSync(imagesDir, { recursive: true });\n  }\n\n  console.log(`\\n📥 Downloading images for ${article.title} (${article.version})`);\n  console.log(`   URL: ${article.url}`);\n  console.log(`   Target: ${imagesDir}`);\n\n  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });\n  const page = await browser.newPage();\n\n  console.log('   Navigating to article...');\n  await page.goto(article.url, {\n    waitUntil: 'networkidle',\n    timeout: 60000\n  });\n\n  // Scroll to load all content\n  console.log('   Scrolling to load lazy images...');\n  await page.evaluate(async () => {\n    const scrollHeight = document.documentElement.scrollHeight;\n    const viewportHeight = window.innerHeight;\n    const scrollSteps = Math.ceil(scrollHeight / viewportHeight);\n    for (let i = 0; i < scrollSteps; i++) {\n      window.scrollTo(0, i * viewportHeight);\n      await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));\n    }\n    window.scrollTo(0, 0);\n  });\n\n  await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n\n  // Extract figure images (those within <figure> elements)\n  console.log('   Extracting figure images...');\n  const figures = await page.$$eval('.article-formatted-body figure', elements =>\n    elements.map(figure => {\n      const img = figure.querySelector('img');\n      const figcaption = figure.querySelector('figcaption');\n      if (!img) return null;\n\n      // Extract figure number from caption (try multiple languages)\n      const captionText = figcaption?.innerText || '';\n      // Match \"Figure X\", \"Рис. X\", \"Рисунок X\"\n      const figureMatch = captionText.match(/(?:Figure|Рис\\.?|Рисунок)\\s*(\\d+)/i);\n      const figureNum = figureMatch ? parseInt(figureMatch[1]) : null;\n\n      return {\n        figureNum,\n        src: img.src,\n        alt: img.alt,\n        caption: captionText\n      };\n    }).filter(f => f !== null && f.src && !f.src.includes('.svg'))\n  );\n\n  console.log(`   Found ${figures.length} figure images`);\n\n  await browser.close();\n\n  // Download each figure image\n  const downloadedImages = [];\n  let sequentialNum = 0;\n  for (const figure of figures) {\n    sequentialNum++;\n    // Use figure number from caption if available, otherwise use sequential number\n    const figNum = figure.figureNum || sequentialNum;\n\n    const ext = figure.src.includes('.jpeg') || figure.src.includes('.jpg') ? 'jpg' : 'png';\n    const filename = `figure-${figNum}.${ext}`;\n    const filepath = join(imagesDir, filename);\n\n    console.log(`   Downloading Figure ${figNum}...`);\n\n    try {\n      await downloadFile(figure.src, filepath);\n      downloadedImages.push({\n        figureNum: figNum,\n        filename,\n        caption: figure.caption\n      });\n      console.log(`     ✓ Saved as ${filename}`);\n    } catch (err) {\n      console.error(`     ✗ Failed: ${err.message}`);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Save image metadata\n  if (downloadedImages.length > 0) {\n    writeFileSync(\n      join(imagesDir, 'metadata.json'),\n      JSON.stringify(downloadedImages, null, 2)\n    );\n  }\n\n  console.log(`\\n   ✅ Downloaded ${downloadedImages.length} images for ${article.version}`);\n  return downloadedImages;\n}\n\n/**\n * Capture a screenshot of the article\n */\nasync function captureScreenshot(article) {\n  const archivePath = join(ROOT_DIR, article.archivePath);\n  const screenshotPath = join(archivePath, article.screenshotFile);\n\n  console.log(`\\n📸 Capturing screenshot for ${article.title} (${article.version})`);\n  console.log(`   URL: ${article.url}`);\n  console.log(`   Target: ${screenshotPath}`);\n\n  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });\n  const context = await browser.newContext({\n    viewport: { width: 1920, height: 1080 },\n    userAgent: 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36'\n  });\n  const page = await context.newPage();\n\n  console.log('   Navigating to article...');\n  await page.goto(article.url, {\n    waitUntil: 'networkidle',\n    timeout: 60000\n  });\n\n  // Multi-pass scrolling to load all lazy content\n  console.log('   Loading all content (3-pass scroll)...');\n  for (let pass = 0; pass < 3; pass++) {\n    await page.evaluate(async () => {\n      const scrollHeight = document.documentElement.scrollHeight;\n      const viewportHeight = window.innerHeight;\n      const scrollSteps = Math.ceil(scrollHeight / viewportHeight);\n      for (let i = 0; i < scrollSteps; i++) {\n        window.scrollTo(0, i * viewportHeight);\n        await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 200));\n      }\n      window.scrollTo(0, 0);\n    });\n    await page.waitForTimeout(1000);\n  }\n\n  // Wait for images to load\n  console.log('   Waiting for images to load...');\n  await page.waitForTimeout(3000);\n\n  // Take full page screenshot\n  console.log('   Taking screenshot...');\n  await page.screenshot({\n    path: screenshotPath,\n    fullPage: true\n  });\n\n  await browser.close();\n\n  const stats = fs.statSync(screenshotPath);\n  console.log(`   ✅ Screenshot saved: ${(stats.size / 1024 / 1024).toFixed(1)} MB`);\n}\n\n/**\n * Main function\n */\nasync function main() {\n  const options = parseArgs();\n\n  // Show help if no action specified\n  if (!options.images && !options.screenshot) {\n    console.log(`\nUsage: node scripts/download.mjs [version] [options]\n\nOptions:\n  --images      Download figure images from the article\n  --screenshot  Capture a full-page screenshot\n  --all         Apply to all articles\n\nExamples:\n  node scripts/download.mjs 0.0.2 --images\n  node scripts/download.mjs 0.0.1 --screenshot\n  node scripts/download.mjs --all --images\n`);\n    process.exit(0);\n  }\n\n  // Get articles to process\n  let articles = [];\n  if (options.all) {\n    articles = getAllArticles();\n  } else if (options.version) {\n    articles = [getArticle(options.version)];\n  } else {\n    console.error('Error: Please specify a version or use --all');\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  console.log('🚀 Download Script');\n  console.log('==================');\n\n  for (const article of articles) {\n    try {\n      if (options.images) {\n        await downloadImages(article);\n      }\n      if (options.screenshot) {\n        await captureScreenshot(article);\n      }\n    } catch (error) {\n      console.error(`\\n❌ Error processing ${article.version}:`, error.message);\n    }\n  }\n\n  console.log('\\n✅ Done!');\n}\n\nmain().catch(console.error);\n",

      "startLine": 1,
      "totalLines": 307
    }
  }

Along the way, we drew inspiration from the workings of our own associative memory and associative thought processes.\n37\t\n38\t### Relational Algebra\n39\t\n40\t[Relational algebra](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Relational_algebra) and the [relational model](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Relational_model) are based on the concepts of [relations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Relation_(database)) and [n-tuples](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tuple)."

We believe that the concepts discussed here can serve as inspiration across a wide range of scientific disciplines.\n\nOur goal was to create a self-contained text that guides you through each topic in a clear, logical order. Throughout the article, you’ll find links to [Wikipedia](https://www.wikipedia.org) for those who wish to explore specific terms or concepts in more depth — but this is entirely optional. The text is intended to be easily understood when read from start to finish.\n\nEvery symbol and formula is explained individually, with concise definitions provided where needed. We’ve also included images to help illustrate key ideas. If you come across anything that isn’t clear, please let us know so we can improve it.\n\n## Comparison of theories\n\nTo quickly dive in, we begin by comparing the mathematical foundations of the two most popular [data models](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Data_model) with that of the [associative model of data](https://web.archive.org/web/20210814063207/https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Associative_model_of_data).\n\nIn the course of our research, we discovered that traditional theories were sometimes overly complex or redundant, while at other times they imposed too many artificial constraints.\n\nThis overall lack of flexibility, adaptability, and universality motivated us to search for a simpler yet all-encompassing informational theory and a data storage model that future artificial intelligence could easily understand and effectively utilize. Along the way, we drew inspiration from the workings of our own associative memory and associative thought processes.\n\n### Relational Algebra\n\n[Relational algebra](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Relational_algebra) and the [relational model](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Relational_model) are based on the concepts of [relations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Relation_(database)) and [n-tuples](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tuple).",

Using n-tuples in a relational model, you can exactly represent any possible data structure. But we are not sure, you need n-tuples for that at all. For example, each n-tuple can be represented as nested ordered pairs (2-tuples). You also don’t often see that columns in tables are used as indexes for sequence (n-tuple) elements. In some databases, you're even prohibited to use more than 32 columns in a table and its row (n-tuple). So the N is usually less than 32. So there are no real n-tuples in this case, even in relational databases of today.\n34\t\n35\t### Directed graph definition\n36\t\n37\tDirected graph and graphs in general are based on concepts of vertex and edge (2-tuple).\n38\t\n39\tA [directed graph](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Directed_graph) G is defined as an ordered pair *G*(*V*, E):\n40\t"

Using n-tuples in a relational model, you can exactly represent any possible data structure. But we are not sure, you need n-tuples for that at all. For example, each n-tuple can be represented as nested ordered pairs (2-tuples). You also don’t often see that columns in tables are used as indexes for sequence (n-tuple) elements. In some databases, you're even prohibited to use more than 32 columns in a table and its row (n-tuple). So the N is usually less than 32. So there are no real n-tuples in this case, even in relational databases of today.\n\n### Directed graph definition\n\nDirected graph and graphs in general are based on concepts of vertex and edge (2-tuple).\n\nA [directed graph](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Directed_graph) G is defined as an ordered pair *G*(*V*, E):\n",

    "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n\n/**\n * Script to download article content from web pages and convert to markdown\n *\n * This script extracts article content from Habr and converts it to markdown format.\n * It handles:\n * - Title extraction\n * - Headings (h2, h3, h4)\n * - Paragraphs with inline links\n * - Code blocks with syntax highlighting (preserves newlines from <br> tags)\n * - Lists (ordered and unordered)\n * - Blockquotes\n * - Images and figures with captions\n * - Links preservation\n * - LaTeX math formulas (extracted from img.formula elements' `source` attribute)\n *\n * FORMULA EXTRACTION:\n * Habr renders formulas as SVG/PNG images with class \"formula\". The original LaTeX\n * source code is stored in the `source` attribute of these img elements. This script\n * automatically extracts and converts them to proper LaTeX markdown format ($...$).\n *\n * Usage:\n *   node scripts/download-article.mjs [version]\n *   node scripts/download-article.mjs --all\n *\n * Examples:\n *   node scripts/download-article.mjs 0.0.1\n *   node scripts/download-article.mjs --all\n */\n\nimport { chromium } from 'playwright';\nimport { writeFileSync, mkdirSync, existsSync } from 'fs';\nimport { fileURLToPath } from 'url';\nimport { dirname, join } from 'path';\nimport { getArticle, getAllArticles } from './articles-config.mjs';\n\nconst __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);\nconst __dirname = dirname(__filename);\nconst ROOT_DIR = join(__dirname, '..');\n\n/**\n * Parse command line arguments\n */\nfunction parseArgs() {\n  const args = process.argv.slice(2);\n  const options = {\n    version: null,\n    all: false,\n    dryRun: false,\n    verbose: false,\n    outputFile: 'article.md'  // Default output file, can be changed with --downloaded\n  };\n\n  for (const arg of args) {\n    if (arg === '--all') {\n      options.all = true;\n    } else if (arg === '--dry-run') {\n      options.dryRun = true;\n    } else if (arg === '--verbose' || arg === '-v') {\n      options.verbose = true;\n    } else if (arg === '--downloaded') {\n      options.outputFile = 'downloaded.md';\n    } else if (!arg.startsWith('-')) {\n      options.version = arg;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return options;\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract article content from web page and convert to markdown\n */\nasync function extractArticleContent(article, verbose = false) {\n  if (verbose) console.log('   Loading web page:', article.url);\n\n  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });\n  const context = await browser.newContext({\n    viewport: { width: 1920, height: 1080 },\n    userAgent: 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36'\n  });\n  const page = await context.newPage();\n\n  // Navigate to the page\n  await page.goto(article.url, {\n    waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded',\n    timeout: 120000\n  });\n\n  // Wait for article body to appear\n  await page.waitForSelector('.article-formatted-body', { timeout: 30000 });\n\n  // Scroll through the page to trigger lazy loading\n  await page.evaluate(async () => {\n    const scrollHeight = document.documentElement.scrollHeight;\n    const viewportHeight = window.innerHeight;\n    const scrollSteps = Math.ceil(scrollHeight / viewportHeight);\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < scrollSteps; i++) {\n      window.scrollTo(0, i * viewportHeight);\n      await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));\n    }\n    window.scrollTo(0, 0);\n  });\n\n  // Wait for dynamic content\n  await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n\n  // Extract article metadata from the page header area\n  const metadata = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const meta = {};\n\n    // Author\n    const authorEl = document.querySelector('.tm-user-info__username');\n    if (authorEl) {\n      meta.author = authorEl.innerText.trim();\n      meta.authorUrl = authorEl.href || null;\n    }\n\n    // Publication date\n    const timeEl = document.querySelector('time[datetime]');\n    if (timeEl) {\n      meta.publishDate = timeEl.getAttribute('datetime');\n      meta.publishDateText = timeEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // Reading time\n    const readTimeEl = document.querySelector('.tm-article-reading-time__label');\n    if (readTimeEl) {\n      meta.readingTime = readTimeEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // Difficulty level\n    const diffEl = document.querySelector('.tm-article-complexity__label');\n    if (diffEl) {\n      meta.difficulty = diffEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // Views\n    const viewsEl = document.querySelector('.tm-icon-counter__value');\n    if (viewsEl) {\n      meta.views = viewsEl.getAttribute('title') || viewsEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // Hubs (use specific hub link selector to avoid duplicates)\n    const hubEls = document.querySelectorAll('.tm-publication-hub__link');\n    meta.hubs = Array.from(hubEls).map(el => {\n      // Get only the first span text (hub name), not the asterisk\n      const nameSpan = el.querySelector('span:first-child');\n      return nameSpan ? nameSpan.innerText.trim() : el.innerText.trim().replace(/\\s*\\*\\s*$/, '');\n    });\n\n    // Tags from meta keywords\n    const keywordsMeta = document.querySelector('meta[name=\"keywords\"]');\n    if (keywordsMeta) {\n      const content = keywordsMeta.getAttribute('content');\n      if (content) {\n        meta.tags = content.split(',').map(t => t.trim()).filter(Boolean);\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Translation / original author info\n    const originEl = document.querySelector('.tm-article-presenter__origin');\n    if (originEl) {\n      meta.originalAuthor = originEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // LD+JSON structured data for additional metadata\n    const ldJsonScript = document.querySelector('script[type=\"application/ld+json\"]');\n    if (ldJsonScript) {\n      try {\n        const ldData = JSON.parse(ldJsonScript.textContent);\n        if (ldData.dateModified) meta.dateModified = ldData.dateModified;\n        if (ldData.author?.name) meta.authorFullName = ldData.author.name;\n      } catch (e) {\n        // ignore parse errors\n      }\n    }\n\n    return meta;\n  });\n\n  if (verbose) {\n    console.log('   Metadata extracted:', JSON.stringify(metadata, null, 2));\n  }\n\n  // Extract article content as structured data with HTML processing\n  const content = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const articleBody = document.querySelector('.article-formatted-body');\n    if (!articleBody) return null;\n\n    // Get article title\n    const titleEl = document.querySelector('article h1');\n    const title = titleEl ? titleEl.innerText.trim() : '';\n\n    /**\n     * Convert an HTML element to markdown, preserving links and formatting\n     */\n    function nodeToMarkdown(node, context = {}) {\n      if (node.nodeType === Node.TEXT_NODE) {\n        return node.textContent;\n      }\n\n      if (node.nodeType !== Node.ELEMENT_NODE) return '';\n\n      const tag = node.tagName.toLowerCase();\n\n      // Skip script, style, etc.\n      if (['script', 'style', 'noscript', 'svg'].includes(tag)) return '';\n\n      // Handle links\n      if (tag === 'a') {\n        const href = node.getAttribute('href');\n        const text = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n        if (href && text) {\n          return `[${text}](${href})`;\n        }\n        return text;\n      }\n\n      // Handle bold\n      if (tag === 'strong' || tag === 'b') {\n        const text = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n        return text ? `**${text}**` : '';\n      }\n\n      // Handle italic\n      if (tag === 'em' || tag === 'i') {\n        const text = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n        return text ? `*${text}*` : '';\n      }\n\n      // Handle inline code\n      if (tag === 'code' && !node.closest('pre')) {\n        const text = node.textContent;\n        return text ? `\\`${text}\\`` : '';\n      }\n\n      // Handle line breaks\n      if (tag === 'br') {\n        return '\\n';\n      }\n\n      // Handle subscript/superscript (common in math)\n      if (tag === 'sub') {\n        return `₍${node.textContent}₎`;\n      }\n      if (tag === 'sup') {\n        return `^${node.textContent}`;\n      }\n\n      // Handle formula images (Habr stores LaTeX in `source` attribute)\n      if (tag === 'img' && node.classList.contains('formula')) {\n        const source = node.getAttribute('source');\n        if (source) {\n          // Inline formula - wrap in single $\n          return `$${source}$`;\n        }\n        // Fallback to alt text\n        const alt = node.getAttribute('alt');\n        if (alt) {\n          return `$${alt}$`;\n        }\n        return '';\n      }\n\n      // Handle math elements (KaTeX/MathJax)\n      if (node.classList.contains('katex') || node.classList.contains('math') ||\n          tag === 'mjx-container' || node.classList.contains('MathJax')) {\n        const annotation = node.querySelector('annotation[encoding=\"application/x-tex\"]');\n        if (annotation) {\n          return annotation.textContent;\n        }\n        // Try to get the LaTeX from data attributes\n        const tex = node.getAttribute('data-tex') || node.getAttribute('data-latex');\n        if (tex) return tex;\n        // Fallback to text content cleaned up\n        return node.textContent.trim();\n      }\n\n      // Handle spans (just process children)\n      if (tag === 'span') {\n        return nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n      }\n\n      // Default: process children\n      return nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n    }\n\n    function nodeToMarkdownChildren(node) {\n      let result = '';\n      for (const child of node.childNodes) {\n        result += nodeToMarkdown(child);\n      }\n      return result;\n    }\n\n    // Process all elements in order\n    const elements = [];\n    let figureIndex = 0;\n\n    const processElement = (node) => {\n      if (node.nodeType !== Node.ELEMENT_NODE) return;\n\n      const tag = node.tagName.toLowerCase();\n\n      // Skip certain elements\n      if (['script', 'style', 'noscript'].includes(tag)) return;\n\n      // Handle headings\n      if (['h1', 'h2', 'h3', 'h4', 'h5', 'h6'].includes(tag)) {\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'heading',\n          level: parseInt(tag[1]),\n          content: nodeToMarkdownChildren(node).trim()\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle paragraphs\n      if (tag === 'p') {\n        const content = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node).trim();\n        if (content) {\n          elements.push({\n            type: 'paragraph',\n            content: content\n          });\n        }\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle code blocks\n      if (tag === 'pre') {\n        const codeEl = node.querySelector('code');\n        // Get innerHTML and convert <br> to newlines, then strip remaining HTML\n        let codeHTML = codeEl ? codeEl.innerHTML : node.innerHTML;\n        // Convert <br> and <br/> to newlines\n        let code = codeHTML.replace(/<br\\s*\\/?>/gi, '\\n');\n        // Strip remaining HTML tags\n        const temp = document.createElement('div');\n        temp.innerHTML = code;\n        code = temp.textContent || temp.innerText;\n        const language = codeEl?.className?.match(/language-(\\w+)/)?.[1] || '';\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'code',\n          language,\n          content: code.trim()\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle blockquotes\n      if (tag === 'blockquote') {\n        const content = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node).trim();\n\n        // Check if the blockquote contains only a formula (common pattern in articles)\n        // Pattern: starts and ends with $ and mostly contains LaTeX content\n        // Allow for some whitespace around the formula\n        const formulaOnlyMatch = content.match(/^\\s*\\$([^$]+)\\$\\s*$/);\n        if (formulaOnlyMatch) {\n          // This is a formula-only blockquote, preserve as blockquote with block formula\n          // Output will be: > $$formula$$\n          const formula = formulaOnlyMatch[1].trim();\n          elements.push({\n            type: 'blockquote-math',\n            content: formula\n          });\n          return;\n        }\n\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'blockquote',\n          content: content\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle unordered lists\n      if (tag === 'ul') {\n        const items = Array.from(node.querySelectorAll(':scope > li')).map(li =>\n          nodeToMarkdownChildren(li).trim()\n        );\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'unordered-list',\n          items: items.filter(item => item)\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle ordered lists\n      if (tag === 'ol') {\n        const items = Array.from(node.querySelectorAll(':scope > li')).map(li =>\n          nodeToMarkdownChildren(li).trim()\n        );\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'ordered-list',\n          items: items.filter(item => item)\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle figures (images with captions)\n      if (tag === 'figure') {\n        figureIndex++;\n        const img = node.querySelector('img');\n        const figcaption = node.querySelector('figcaption');\n        if (img) {\n          elements.push({\n            type: 'figure',\n            index: figureIndex,\n            src: img.src,\n            alt: img.alt || '',\n            caption: figcaption ? nodeToMarkdownChildren(figcaption).trim() : ''\n          });\n        }\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle standalone images (but not formula images - those are inline)\n      if (tag === 'img' && !node.closest('figure')) {\n        // Check if this is a formula image - handle as block formula if standalone\n        if (node.classList.contains('formula')) {\n          const source = node.getAttribute('source');\n          if (source) {\n            // Standalone formula - treat as block formula\n            elements.push({\n              type: 'math-block',\n              content: source\n            });\n            return;\n          }\n        }\n        // Regular image\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'image',\n          src: node.src,\n          alt: node.alt || ''\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle horizontal rules\n      if (tag === 'hr') {\n        elements.push({ type: 'hr' });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle div elements that might contain math blocks\n      if (tag === 'div') {\n        // Check for math block\n        const mathEl = node.querySelector('.katex-display, .math-display, mjx-container[display=\"true\"]');\n        if (mathEl) {\n          const annotation = mathEl.querySelector('annotation[encoding=\"application/x-tex\"]');\n          const tex = annotation ? annotation.textContent :\n                     (mathEl.getAttribute('data-tex') || mathEl.textContent);\n          if (tex) {\n            elements.push({\n              type: 'math-block',\n              content: tex.trim()\n            });\n          }\n          return;\n        }\n\n        // For other divs, process children\n        for (const child of node.children) {\n          processElement(child);\n        }\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // For other block elements, try to process children\n      if (['section', 'article', 'main', 'aside', 'header', 'footer', 'details'].includes(tag)) {\n        for (const child of node.children) {\n          processElement(child);\n        }\n      }\n    };\n\n    for (const child of articleBody.children) {\n      processElement(child);\n    }\n\n    return { title, elements };\n  });\n\n  await browser.close();\n\n  if (content) {\n    content.metadata = metadata;\n  }\n\n  return content;\n}\n\n/**\n * Post-process markdown to fix formatting issues\n */\nfunction postProcessMarkdown(markdown) {\n  let result = markdown;\n\n  // Unicode character normalization to match article.md style\n  // Normalize curly quotes to straight quotes\n  result = result.replace(/['']/g, \"'\");\n  result = result.replace(/[\"\"]/g, '\"');\n\n  // Normalize em-dash and en-dash to regular dash with spaces\n  result = result.replace(/—/g, ' — ');  // Keep em-dash with spaces for readability\n  result = result.replace(/–/g, '-');     // Convert en-dash to hyphen\n\n  // Normalize ellipsis\n  result = result.replace(/…/g, '...');\n\n  // Fix spacing around inline LaTeX formulas\n  // Pattern: word$formula$word should become word $formula$ word\n  // We need to run multiple passes to handle all cases\n\n  // Pass 1: Add space after formula when followed by word character\n  // Match: $formula$word -> $formula$ word\n  result = result.replace(/(\\$[^$\\n]+\\$)([a-zA-Zа-яА-ЯёЁ])/g, (match, formula, nextChar) => {\n    return formula + ' ' + nextChar;\n  });\n\n  // Pass 2: Add space before formula when preceded by word character\n  // Match: word$formula$ -> word $formula$\n  result = result.replace(/([a-zA-Zа-яА-ЯёЁ])(\\$[^$\\n]+\\$)/g, (match, prevChar, formula) => {\n    return prevChar + ' ' + formula;\n  });\n\n  // Fix multiple inline formulas on the same line (common pattern from inline extraction)\n  // Pattern: $formula1$$formula2$ -> $formula1$\\n\\n$formula2$\n  result = result.replace(/(\\$[^$\\n]+\\$)\\$([^$\\n]+\\$)/g, (match, formula1, formula2) => {\n    return formula1 + '\\n\\n$' + formula2;\n  });\n\n  // Note: We removed the \"clean up whitespace inside formulas\" regexes because they\n  // were incorrectly matching and removing spaces BETWEEN formula and adjacent text.\n  // The formulas from the source already have correct internal spacing.\n\n  // Fix double spaces (but not in code blocks)\n  result = result.replace(/([^\\n`])  +/g, (match, char) => {\n    return char + ' ';\n  });\n\n  // Clean up extra spaces around em-dashes that we may have introduced\n  result = result.replace(/\\s+—\\s+/g, ' — ');\n\n  // Fix stray standalone $ signs that might have been left from parsing\n  // Pattern: $\\n\\n$ or just a standalone $ on its own line should be removed\n  result = result.replace(/^\\$\\s*$/gm, '');\n\n  return result;\n}\n\n/**\n * Format metadata as a markdown block to be placed after the title\n */\nfunction formatMetadataBlock(metadata) {\n  if (!metadata) return [];\n\n  const lines = [];\n\n  // Author line\n  if (metadata.author) {\n    const authorName = metadata.authorFullName\n      ? `${metadata.authorFullName} (${metadata.author})`\n      : metadata.author;\n    const authorLink = metadata.authorUrl\n      ? `[${authorName}](${metadata.authorUrl})`\n      : authorName;\n    lines.push(`**Author:** ${authorLink}`);\n  }\n\n  // Original author (for translations)\n  if (metadata.originalAuthor) {\n    lines.push(`**${metadata.originalAuthor}**`);\n  }\n\n  // Publication date\n  if (metadata.publishDate) {\n    const date = new Date(metadata.publishDate);\n    const formatted = date.toLocaleDateString('en-US', {\n      year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric'\n    });\n    let dateLine = `**Published:** ${formatted}`;\n    if (metadata.dateModified) {\n      const modDate = new Date(metadata.dateModified);\n      const modFormatted = modDate.toLocaleDateString('en-US', {\n        year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric'\n      });\n      if (modFormatted !== formatted) {\n        dateLine += ` (updated ${modFormatted})`;\n      }\n    }\n    lines.push(dateLine);\n  }\n\n  // Reading time and difficulty\n  const infoItems = [];\n  if (metadata.readingTime) infoItems.push(`Reading time: ${metadata.readingTime}`);\n  if (metadata.difficulty) infoItems.push(`Difficulty: ${metadata.difficulty}`);\n  if (metadata.views) infoItems.push(`Views: ${metadata.views}`);\n  if (infoItems.length > 0) {\n    lines.push(`**${infoItems.join(' | ')}**`);\n  }\n\n  // Hubs\n  if (metadata.hubs && metadata.hubs.length > 0) {\n    lines.push(`**Hubs:** ${metadata.hubs.join(', ')}`);\n  }\n\n  // Tags\n  if (metadata.tags && metadata.tags.length > 0) {\n    lines.push(`**Tags:** ${metadata.tags.join(', ')}`);\n  }\n\n  return lines;\n}\n\n/**\n * Convert extracted content to markdown\n */\nfunction contentToMarkdown(content, article) {\n  if (!content) return '';\n\n  const lines = [];\n\n  // Add title\n  if (content.title) {\n    lines.push(`# ${content.title}`);\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n\n  // Add metadata block after title\n  const metadataLines = formatMetadataBlock(content.metadata);\n  if (metadataLines.length > 0) {\n    for (const line of metadataLines) {\n      lines.push(line);\n    }\n    lines.push('');\n    lines.push('---');\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n\n  let imageIndex = 1;\n\n  for (const element of content.elements) {\n    switch (element.type) {\n      case 'heading':\n        const prefix = '#'.repeat(element.level);\n        lines.push(`${prefix} ${element.content}`);\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'paragraph':\n        if (element.content) {\n          lines.push(element.content);\n          lines.push('');\n        }\n        break;\n\n      case 'code':\n        lines.push('```' + (element.language || ''));\n        lines.push(element.content);\n        lines.push('```');\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'blockquote':\n        const quoteLines = element.content.split('\\n');\n        for (const line of quoteLines) {\n          lines.push(`> ${line}`);\n        }\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'unordered-list':\n        for (const item of element.items) {\n          // Handle multi-line list items\n          const itemLines = item.split('\\n');\n          lines.push(`- ${itemLines[0]}`);\n          for (let i = 1; i < itemLines.length; i++) {\n            lines.push(`  ${itemLines[i]}`);\n          }\n        }\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'ordered-list':\n        element.items.forEach((item, i) => {\n          const itemLines = item.split('\\n');\n          lines.push(`${i + 1}. ${itemLines[0]}`);\n          for (let j = 1; j < itemLines.length; j++) {\n            lines.push(`   ${itemLines[j]}`);\n          }\n        });\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'figure':\n        // Use local image path with figure number\n        const figureMatch = element.caption.match(/(?:Figure|Рис\\.?|Рисунок)\\s*(\\d+)/i);\n        const figNum = figureMatch ? figureMatch[1] : element.index;\n        const ext = element.src.includes('.jpeg') || element.src.includes('.jpg') ? 'jpg' : 'png';\n\n        // Simple alt text format: \"Figure N\" only, not the full caption\n        // This matches the article.md format\n        const simpleAltText = `Figure ${figNum}`;\n        lines.push(`![${simpleAltText}](images/figure-${figNum}.${ext})`);\n        if (element.caption) {\n          // Caption already includes bold formatting from extraction (e.g., **Figure 1.** ...)\n          // Don't wrap it again in bold, just output as-is\n          lines.push('');\n          lines.push(element.caption);\n        }\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'image':\n        // Use local path format\n        const imgExt = element.src.includes('.jpeg') || element.src.includes('.jpg') ? 'jpg' : 'png';\n        lines.push(`![${element.alt}](images/image-${String(imageIndex).padStart(2, '0')}.${imgExt})`);\n        lines.push('');\n        imageIndex++;\n        break;\n\n      case 'math-block':\n        lines.push('$$' + element.content + '$$');\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'blockquote-math':\n        // Formula in a blockquote - preserve blockquote formatting with block formula\n        lines.push('> $$' + element.content + '$$');\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'hr':\n        lines.push('---');\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n    }\n  }\n\n  const rawMarkdown = lines.join('\\n').trim() + '\\n';\n  return postProcessMarkdown(rawMarkdown);\n}\n\n/**\n * Download article and save as markdown\n */\nasync function downloadArticle(article, options) {\n  const archivePath = join(ROOT_DIR, article.archivePath);\n  const outputFileName = options.outputFile || article.markdownFile;\n  const markdownPath = join(archivePath, outputFileName);\n\n  console.log(`\\n📥 Downloading ${article.title} (${article.version})`);\n  console.log('='.repeat(70));\n  console.log(`   URL: ${article.url}`);\n  console.log(`   Target: ${markdownPath}`);\n\n  // Ensure archive directory exists\n  if (!existsSync(archivePath)) {\n    mkdirSync(archivePath, { recursive: true });\n    console.log(`   Created directory: ${archivePath}`);\n  }\n\n  // Extract content from web page\n  console.log('   Extracting content from web page...');\n  const content = await extractArticleContent(article, options.verbose);\n\n  if (!content) {\n    console.error('   ❌ Failed to extract article content');\n    return { success: false, error: 'Failed to extract content' };\n  }\n\n  console.log(`   ✅ Extracted ${content.elements.length} elements`);\n\n  // Convert to markdown\n  console.log('   Converting to markdown...');\n  const markdown = contentToMarkdown(content, article);\n\n  if (options.dryRun) {\n    console.log('   [DRY RUN] Would save markdown file');\n    console.log(`   Preview (first 500 chars):\\n${markdown.substring(0, 500)}...`);\n    return { success: true, dryRun: true };\n  }\n\n  // Save markdown file\n  writeFileSync(markdownPath, markdown, 'utf-8');\n  console.log(`   ✅ Saved ${markdownPath}`);\n  console.log(`   File size: ${(markdown.length / 1024).toFixed(1)} KB`);\n\n  return { success: true, path: markdownPath, size: markdown.length };\n}\n\n/**\n * Main function\n */\nasync function main() {\n  const options = parseArgs();\n\n  // Show help if no version specified\n  if (!options.all && !options.version) {\n    console.log(`\nUsage: node scripts/download-article.mjs [version] [options]\n\nOptions:\n  --all         Download all articles\n  --downloaded  Save as downloaded.md instead of article.md\n  --dry-run     Show what would be done without making changes\n  --verbose     Show detailed output\n\nExamples:\n  node scripts/download-article.mjs 0.0.1\n  node scripts/download-article.mjs --all\n  node scripts/download-article.mjs --all --downloaded\n  node scripts/download-article.mjs 0.0.2 --dry-run\n`);\n    process.exit(0);\n  }\n\n  // Get articles to process\n  let articles = [];\n  if (options.all) {\n    articles = getAllArticles();\n  } else if (options.version) {\n    articles = [getArticle(options.version)];\n  }\n\n  console.log('🚀 Article Download Script');\n  console.log('==========================');\n  if (options.dryRun) {\n    console.log('⚠️  DRY RUN MODE - No files will be created\\n');\n  }\n\n  const results = [];\n\n  for (const article of articles) {\n    try {\n      const result = await downloadArticle(article, options);\n      results.push({ article, ...result });\n    } catch (error) {\n      console.error(`\\n❌ Error downloading ${article.version}:`, error.message);\n      results.push({ article, success: false, error: error.message });\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Print summary\n  console.log('\\n' + '='.repeat(70));\n  console.log('📊 DOWNLOAD SUMMARY');\n  console.log('='.repeat(70));\n\n  for (const result of results) {\n    const status = result.success ? '✅' : '❌';\n    const details = result.success\n      ? (result.dryRun ? 'dry run' : `${(result.size / 1024).toFixed(1)} KB`)\n      : result.error;\n    console.log(`   ${status} ${result.article.version}: ${details}`);\n  }\n\n  console.log('\\n' + '='.repeat(70));\n  process.exit(results.every(r => r.success) ? 0 : 1);\n}\n\nmain().catch(error => {\n  console.error('❌ Error:', error);\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n",

    "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n\n/**\n * Script to download article content from web pages and convert to markdown\n *\n * This script extracts article content from Habr and converts it to markdown format.\n * It handles:\n * - Title extraction\n * - Headings (h2, h3, h4)\n * - Paragraphs with inline links\n * - Code blocks with syntax highlighting (preserves newlines from <br> tags)\n * - Lists (ordered and unordered)\n * - Blockquotes\n * - Images and figures with captions\n * - Links preservation\n * - LaTeX math formulas (extracted from img.formula elements' `source` attribute)\n *\n * FORMULA EXTRACTION:\n * Habr renders formulas as SVG/PNG images with class \"formula\". The original LaTeX\n * source code is stored in the `source` attribute of these img elements. This script\n * automatically extracts and converts them to proper LaTeX markdown format ($...$).\n *\n * Usage:\n *   node scripts/download-article.mjs [version]\n *   node scripts/download-article.mjs --all\n *\n * Examples:\n *   node scripts/download-article.mjs 0.0.1\n *   node scripts/download-article.mjs --all\n */\n\nimport { chromium } from 'playwright';\nimport { writeFileSync, mkdirSync, existsSync } from 'fs';\nimport { fileURLToPath } from 'url';\nimport { dirname, join } from 'path';\nimport { getArticle, getAllArticles } from './articles-config.mjs';\n\nconst __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);\nconst __dirname = dirname(__filename);\nconst ROOT_DIR = join(__dirname, '..');\n\n/**\n * Parse command line arguments\n */\nfunction parseArgs() {\n  const args = process.argv.slice(2);\n  const options = {\n    version: null,\n    all: false,\n    dryRun: false,\n    verbose: false,\n    outputFile: 'article.md'  // Default output file, can be changed with --downloaded\n  };\n\n  for (const arg of args) {\n    if (arg === '--all') {\n      options.all = true;\n    } else if (arg === '--dry-run') {\n      options.dryRun = true;\n    } else if (arg === '--verbose' || arg === '-v') {\n      options.verbose = true;\n    } else if (arg === '--downloaded') {\n      options.outputFile = 'downloaded.md';\n    } else if (!arg.startsWith('-')) {\n      options.version = arg;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return options;\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract article content from web page and convert to markdown\n */\nasync function extractArticleContent(article, verbose = false) {\n  if (verbose) console.log('   Loading web page:', article.url);\n\n  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });\n  const context = await browser.newContext({\n    viewport: { width: 1920, height: 1080 },\n    userAgent: 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36'\n  });\n  const page = await context.newPage();\n\n  // Navigate to the page\n  await page.goto(article.url, {\n    waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded',\n    timeout: 120000\n  });\n\n  // Wait for article body to appear\n  await page.waitForSelector('.article-formatted-body', { timeout: 30000 });\n\n  // Scroll through the page to trigger lazy loading\n  await page.evaluate(async () => {\n    const scrollHeight = document.documentElement.scrollHeight;\n    const viewportHeight = window.innerHeight;\n    const scrollSteps = Math.ceil(scrollHeight / viewportHeight);\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < scrollSteps; i++) {\n      window.scrollTo(0, i * viewportHeight);\n      await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));\n    }\n    window.scrollTo(0, 0);\n  });\n\n  // Wait for dynamic content\n  await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n\n  // Extract article metadata from the page header area\n  const metadata = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const meta = {};\n\n    // Author\n    const authorEl = document.querySelector('.tm-user-info__username');\n    if (authorEl) {\n      meta.author = authorEl.innerText.trim();\n      meta.authorUrl = authorEl.href || null;\n    }\n\n    // Publication date\n    const timeEl = document.querySelector('time[datetime]');\n    if (timeEl) {\n      meta.publishDate = timeEl.getAttribute('datetime');\n      meta.publishDateText = timeEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // Reading time\n    const readTimeEl = document.querySelector('.tm-article-reading-time__label');\n    if (readTimeEl) {\n      meta.readingTime = readTimeEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // Difficulty level\n    const diffEl = document.querySelector('.tm-article-complexity__label');\n    if (diffEl) {\n      meta.difficulty = diffEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // Views\n    const viewsEl = document.querySelector('.tm-icon-counter__value');\n    if (viewsEl) {\n      meta.views = viewsEl.getAttribute('title') || viewsEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // Hubs (use specific hub link selector to avoid duplicates)\n    const hubEls = document.querySelectorAll('.tm-publication-hub__link');\n    meta.hubs = Array.from(hubEls).map(el => {\n      // Get only the first span text (hub name), not the asterisk\n      const nameSpan = el.querySelector('span:first-child');\n      return nameSpan ? nameSpan.innerText.trim() : el.innerText.trim().replace(/\\s*\\*\\s*$/, '');\n    });\n\n    // Tags from meta keywords\n    const keywordsMeta = document.querySelector('meta[name=\"keywords\"]');\n    if (keywordsMeta) {\n      const content = keywordsMeta.getAttribute('content');\n      if (content) {\n        meta.tags = content.split(',').map(t => t.trim()).filter(Boolean);\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Translation badge - detect if article is a translation\n    const translationLabelEl = document.querySelector('.tm-publication-label_variant-translation');\n    if (translationLabelEl) {\n      meta.isTranslation = true;\n      meta.translationLabel = translationLabelEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // Translation / original author info and link to original article\n    const originLinkEl = document.querySelector('.tm-article-presenter__origin-link');\n    if (originLinkEl) {\n      meta.originalArticleUrl = originLinkEl.href || null;\n      // Extract just the author names from the span inside the link\n      const authorSpan = originLinkEl.querySelector('span');\n      if (authorSpan) {\n        meta.originalAuthors = authorSpan.innerText.trim();\n      }\n      // Full text includes the label like \"Original author: ...\"\n      meta.originalAuthorText = originLinkEl.innerText.trim();\n    }\n\n    // LD+JSON structured data for additional metadata\n    const ldJsonScript = document.querySelector('script[type=\"application/ld+json\"]');\n    if (ldJsonScript) {\n      try {\n        const ldData = JSON.parse(ldJsonScript.textContent);\n        if (ldData.dateModified) meta.dateModified = ldData.dateModified;\n        if (ldData.author?.name) meta.authorFullName = ldData.author.name;\n      } catch (e) {\n        // ignore parse errors\n      }\n    }\n\n    return meta;\n  });\n\n  if (verbose) {\n    console.log('   Metadata extracted:', JSON.stringify(metadata, null, 2));\n  }\n\n  // Extract article content as structured data with HTML processing\n  const content = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const articleBody = document.querySelector('.article-formatted-body');\n    if (!articleBody) return null;\n\n    // Get article title\n    const titleEl = document.querySelector('article h1');\n    const title = titleEl ? titleEl.innerText.trim() : '';\n\n    /**\n     * Convert an HTML element to markdown, preserving links and formatting\n     */\n    function nodeToMarkdown(node, context = {}) {\n      if (node.nodeType === Node.TEXT_NODE) {\n        return node.textContent;\n      }\n\n      if (node.nodeType !== Node.ELEMENT_NODE) return '';\n\n      const tag = node.tagName.toLowerCase();\n\n      // Skip script, style, etc.\n      if (['script', 'style', 'noscript', 'svg'].includes(tag)) return '';\n\n      // Handle links\n      if (tag === 'a') {\n        const href = node.getAttribute('href');\n        const text = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n        if (href && text) {\n          return `[${text}](${href})`;\n        }\n        return text;\n      }\n\n      // Handle bold\n      if (tag === 'strong' || tag === 'b') {\n        const text = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n        return text ? `**${text}**` : '';\n      }\n\n      // Handle italic\n      if (tag === 'em' || tag === 'i') {\n        const text = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n        return text ? `*${text}*` : '';\n      }\n\n      // Handle inline code\n      if (tag === 'code' && !node.closest('pre')) {\n        const text = node.textContent;\n        return text ? `\\`${text}\\`` : '';\n      }\n\n      // Handle line breaks\n      if (tag === 'br') {\n        return '\\n';\n      }\n\n      // Handle subscript/superscript (common in math)\n      if (tag === 'sub') {\n        return `₍${node.textContent}₎`;\n      }\n      if (tag === 'sup') {\n        return `^${node.textContent}`;\n      }\n\n      // Handle formula images (Habr stores LaTeX in `source` attribute)\n      if (tag === 'img' && node.classList.contains('formula')) {\n        const source = node.getAttribute('source');\n        if (source) {\n          // Inline formula - wrap in single $\n          return `$${source}$`;\n        }\n        // Fallback to alt text\n        const alt = node.getAttribute('alt');\n        if (alt) {\n          return `$${alt}$`;\n        }\n        return '';\n      }\n\n      // Handle math elements (KaTeX/MathJax)\n      if (node.classList.contains('katex') || node.classList.contains('math') ||\n          tag === 'mjx-container' || node.classList.contains('MathJax')) {\n        const annotation = node.querySelector('annotation[encoding=\"application/x-tex\"]');\n        if (annotation) {\n          return annotation.textContent;\n        }\n        // Try to get the LaTeX from data attributes\n        const tex = node.getAttribute('data-tex') || node.getAttribute('data-latex');\n        if (tex) return tex;\n        // Fallback to text content cleaned up\n        return node.textContent.trim();\n      }\n\n      // Handle spans (just process children)\n      if (tag === 'span') {\n        return nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n      }\n\n      // Default: process children\n      return nodeToMarkdownChildren(node);\n    }\n\n    function nodeToMarkdownChildren(node) {\n      let result = '';\n      for (const child of node.childNodes) {\n        result += nodeToMarkdown(child);\n      }\n      return result;\n    }\n\n    // Process all elements in order\n    const elements = [];\n    let figureIndex = 0;\n\n    const processElement = (node) => {\n      if (node.nodeType !== Node.ELEMENT_NODE) return;\n\n      const tag = node.tagName.toLowerCase();\n\n      // Skip certain elements\n      if (['script', 'style', 'noscript'].includes(tag)) return;\n\n      // Handle headings\n      if (['h1', 'h2', 'h3', 'h4', 'h5', 'h6'].includes(tag)) {\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'heading',\n          level: parseInt(tag[1]),\n          content: nodeToMarkdownChildren(node).trim()\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle paragraphs\n      if (tag === 'p') {\n        const content = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node).trim();\n        if (content) {\n          elements.push({\n            type: 'paragraph',\n            content: content\n          });\n        }\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle code blocks\n      if (tag === 'pre') {\n        const codeEl = node.querySelector('code');\n        // Get innerHTML and convert <br> to newlines, then strip remaining HTML\n        let codeHTML = codeEl ? codeEl.innerHTML : node.innerHTML;\n        // Convert <br> and <br/> to newlines\n        let code = codeHTML.replace(/<br\\s*\\/?>/gi, '\\n');\n        // Strip remaining HTML tags\n        const temp = document.createElement('div');\n        temp.innerHTML = code;\n        code = temp.textContent || temp.innerText;\n        const language = codeEl?.className?.match(/language-(\\w+)/)?.[1] || '';\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'code',\n          language,\n          content: code.trim()\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle blockquotes\n      if (tag === 'blockquote') {\n        const content = nodeToMarkdownChildren(node).trim();\n\n        // Check if the blockquote contains only a formula (common pattern in articles)\n        // Pattern: starts and ends with $ and mostly contains LaTeX content\n        // Allow for some whitespace around the formula\n        const formulaOnlyMatch = content.match(/^\\s*\\$([^$]+)\\$\\s*$/);\n        if (formulaOnlyMatch) {\n          // This is a formula-only blockquote, preserve as blockquote with block formula\n          // Output will be: > $$formula$$\n          const formula = formulaOnlyMatch[1].trim();\n          elements.push({\n            type: 'blockquote-math',\n            content: formula\n          });\n          return;\n        }\n\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'blockquote',\n          content: content\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle unordered lists\n      if (tag === 'ul') {\n        const items = Array.from(node.querySelectorAll(':scope > li')).map(li =>\n          nodeToMarkdownChildren(li).trim()\n        );\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'unordered-list',\n          items: items.filter(item => item)\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle ordered lists\n      if (tag === 'ol') {\n        const items = Array.from(node.querySelectorAll(':scope > li')).map(li =>\n          nodeToMarkdownChildren(li).trim()\n        );\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'ordered-list',\n          items: items.filter(item => item)\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle figures (images with captions)\n      if (tag === 'figure') {\n        figureIndex++;\n        const img = node.querySelector('img');\n        const figcaption = node.querySelector('figcaption');\n        if (img) {\n          elements.push({\n            type: 'figure',\n            index: figureIndex,\n            src: img.src,\n            alt: img.alt || '',\n            caption: figcaption ? nodeToMarkdownChildren(figcaption).trim() : ''\n          });\n        }\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle standalone images (but not formula images - those are inline)\n      if (tag === 'img' && !node.closest('figure')) {\n        // Check if this is a formula image - handle as block formula if standalone\n        if (node.classList.contains('formula')) {\n          const source = node.getAttribute('source');\n          if (source) {\n            // Standalone formula - treat as block formula\n            elements.push({\n              type: 'math-block',\n              content: source\n            });\n            return;\n          }\n        }\n        // Regular image\n        elements.push({\n          type: 'image',\n          src: node.src,\n          alt: node.alt || ''\n        });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle horizontal rules\n      if (tag === 'hr') {\n        elements.push({ type: 'hr' });\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Handle div elements that might contain math blocks\n      if (tag === 'div') {\n        // Check for math block\n        const mathEl = node.querySelector('.katex-display, .math-display, mjx-container[display=\"true\"]');\n        if (mathEl) {\n          const annotation = mathEl.querySelector('annotation[encoding=\"application/x-tex\"]');\n          const tex = annotation ? annotation.textContent :\n                     (mathEl.getAttribute('data-tex') || mathEl.textContent);\n          if (tex) {\n            elements.push({\n              type: 'math-block',\n              content: tex.trim()\n            });\n          }\n          return;\n        }\n\n        // For other divs, process children\n        for (const child of node.children) {\n          processElement(child);\n        }\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // For other block elements, try to process children\n      if (['section', 'article', 'main', 'aside', 'header', 'footer', 'details'].includes(tag)) {\n        for (const child of node.children) {\n          processElement(child);\n        }\n      }\n    };\n\n    for (const child of articleBody.children) {\n      processElement(child);\n    }\n\n    return { title, elements };\n  });\n\n  await browser.close();\n\n  if (content) {\n    content.metadata = metadata;\n  }\n\n  return content;\n}\n\n/**\n * Post-process markdown to fix formatting issues\n */\nfunction postProcessMarkdown(markdown) {\n  let result = markdown;\n\n  // Unicode character normalization to match article.md style\n  // Normalize curly quotes to straight quotes\n  result = result.replace(/['']/g, \"'\");\n  result = result.replace(/[\"\"]/g, '\"');\n\n  // Normalize em-dash and en-dash to regular dash with spaces\n  result = result.replace(/—/g, ' — ');  // Keep em-dash with spaces for readability\n  result = result.replace(/–/g, '-');     // Convert en-dash to hyphen\n\n  // Normalize ellipsis\n  result = result.replace(/…/g, '...');\n\n  // Fix spacing around inline LaTeX formulas\n  // Pattern: word$formula$word should become word $formula$ word\n  // We need to run multiple passes to handle all cases\n\n  // Pass 1: Add space after formula when followed by word character\n  // Match: $formula$word -> $formula$ word\n  result = result.replace(/(\\$[^$\\n]+\\$)([a-zA-Zа-яА-ЯёЁ])/g, (match, formula, nextChar) => {\n    return formula + ' ' + nextChar;\n  });\n\n  // Pass 2: Add space before formula when preceded by word character\n  // Match: word$formula$ -> word $formula$\n  result = result.replace(/([a-zA-Zа-яА-ЯёЁ])(\\$[^$\\n]+\\$)/g, (match, prevChar, formula) => {\n    return prevChar + ' ' + formula;\n  });\n\n  // Fix multiple inline formulas on the same line (common pattern from inline extraction)\n  // Pattern: $formula1$$formula2$ -> $formula1$\\n\\n$formula2$\n  result = result.replace(/(\\$[^$\\n]+\\$)\\$([^$\\n]+\\$)/g, (match, formula1, formula2) => {\n    return formula1 + '\\n\\n$' + formula2;\n  });\n\n  // Note: We removed the \"clean up whitespace inside formulas\" regexes because they\n  // were incorrectly matching and removing spaces BETWEEN formula and adjacent text.\n  // The formulas from the source already have correct internal spacing.\n\n  // Fix double spaces (but not in code blocks)\n  result = result.replace(/([^\\n`])  +/g, (match, char) => {\n    return char + ' ';\n  });\n\n  // Clean up extra spaces around em-dashes that we may have introduced\n  result = result.replace(/\\s+—\\s+/g, ' — ');\n\n  // Fix stray standalone $ signs that might have been left from parsing\n  // Pattern: $\\n\\n$ or just a standalone $ on its own line should be removed\n  result = result.replace(/^\\$\\s*$/gm, '');\n\n  return result;\n}\n\n/**\n * Format metadata as a markdown block to be placed after the title\n */\nfunction formatMetadataBlock(metadata) {\n  if (!metadata) return [];\n\n  const lines = [];\n\n  // Author line\n  if (metadata.author) {\n    const authorName = metadata.authorFullName\n      ? `${metadata.authorFullName} (${metadata.author})`\n      : metadata.author;\n    const authorLink = metadata.authorUrl\n      ? `[${authorName}](${metadata.authorUrl})`\n      : authorName;\n    lines.push(`**Author:** ${authorLink}`);\n  }\n\n  // Original author (for translations)\n  if (metadata.originalAuthor) {\n    lines.push(`**${metadata.originalAuthor}**`);\n  }\n\n  // Publication date\n  if (metadata.publishDate) {\n    const date = new Date(metadata.publishDate);\n    const formatted = date.toLocaleDateString('en-US', {\n      year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric'\n    });\n    let dateLine = `**Published:** ${formatted}`;\n    if (metadata.dateModified) {\n      const modDate = new Date(metadata.dateModified);\n      const modFormatted = modDate.toLocaleDateString('en-US', {\n        year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric'\n      });\n      if (modFormatted !== formatted) {\n        dateLine += ` (updated ${modFormatted})`;\n      }\n    }\n    lines.push(dateLine);\n  }\n\n  // Reading time and difficulty\n  const infoItems = [];\n  if (metadata.readingTime) infoItems.push(`Reading time: ${metadata.readingTime}`);\n  if (metadata.difficulty) infoItems.push(`Difficulty: ${metadata.difficulty}`);\n  if (metadata.views) infoItems.push(`Views: ${metadata.views}`);\n  if (infoItems.length > 0) {\n    lines.push(`**${infoItems.join(' | ')}**`);\n  }\n\n  // Hubs\n  if (metadata.hubs && metadata.hubs.length > 0) {\n    lines.push(`**Hubs:** ${metadata.hubs.join(', ')}`);\n  }\n\n  // Tags\n  if (metadata.tags && metadata.tags.length > 0) {\n    lines.push(`**Tags:** ${metadata.tags.join(', ')}`);\n  }\n\n  return lines;\n}\n\n/**\n * Convert extracted content to markdown\n */\nfunction contentToMarkdown(content, article) {\n  if (!content) return '';\n\n  const lines = [];\n\n  // Add title\n  if (content.title) {\n    lines.push(`# ${content.title}`);\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n\n  // Add metadata block after title\n  const metadataLines = formatMetadataBlock(content.metadata);\n  if (metadataLines.length > 0) {\n    for (const line of metadataLines) {\n      lines.push(line);\n    }\n    lines.push('');\n    lines.push('---');\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n\n  let imageIndex = 1;\n\n  for (const element of content.elements) {\n    switch (element.type) {\n      case 'heading':\n        const prefix = '#'.repeat(element.level);\n        lines.push(`${prefix} ${element.content}`);\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'paragraph':\n        if (element.content) {\n          lines.push(element.content);\n          lines.push('');\n        }\n        break;\n\n      case 'code':\n        lines.push('```' + (element.language || ''));\n        lines.push(element.content);\n        lines.push('```');\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'blockquote':\n        const quoteLines = element.content.split('\\n');\n        for (const line of quoteLines) {\n          lines.push(`> ${line}`);\n        }\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'unordered-list':\n        for (const item of element.items) {\n          // Handle multi-line list items\n          const itemLines = item.split('\\n');\n          lines.push(`- ${itemLines[0]}`);\n          for (let i = 1; i < itemLines.length; i++) {\n            lines.push(`  ${itemLines[i]}`);\n          }\n        }\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'ordered-list':\n        element.items.forEach((item, i) => {\n          const itemLines = item.split('\\n');\n          lines.push(`${i + 1}. ${itemLines[0]}`);\n          for (let j = 1; j < itemLines.length; j++) {\n            lines.push(`   ${itemLines[j]}`);\n          }\n        });\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'figure':\n        // Use local image path with figure number\n        const figureMatch = element.caption.match(/(?:Figure|Рис\\.?|Рисунок)\\s*(\\d+)/i);\n        const figNum = figureMatch ? figureMatch[1] : element.index;\n        const ext = element.src.includes('.jpeg') || element.src.includes('.jpg') ? 'jpg' : 'png';\n\n        // Simple alt text format: \"Figure N\" only, not the full caption\n        // This matches the article.md format\n        const simpleAltText = `Figure ${figNum}`;\n        lines.push(`![${simpleAltText}](images/figure-${figNum}.${ext})`);\n        if (element.caption) {\n          // Caption already includes bold formatting from extraction (e.g., **Figure 1.** ...)\n          // Don't wrap it again in bold, just output as-is\n          lines.push('');\n          lines.push(element.caption);\n        }\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'image':\n        // Use local path format\n        const imgExt = element.src.includes('.jpeg') || element.src.includes('.jpg') ? 'jpg' : 'png';\n        lines.push(`![${element.alt}](images/image-${String(imageIndex).padStart(2, '0')}.${imgExt})`);\n        lines.push('');\n        imageIndex++;\n        break;\n\n      case 'math-block':\n        lines.push('$$' + element.content + '$$');\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'blockquote-math':\n        // Formula in a blockquote - preserve blockquote formatting with block formula\n        lines.push('> $$' + element.content + '$$');\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n\n      case 'hr':\n        lines.push('---');\n        lines.push('');\n        break;\n    }\n  }\n\n  const rawMarkdown = lines.join('\\n').trim() + '\\n';\n  return postProcessMarkdown(rawMarkdown);\n}\n\n/**\n * Download article and save as markdown\n */\nasync function downloadArticle(article, options) {\n  const archivePath = join(ROOT_DIR, article.archivePath);\n  const outputFileName = options.outputFile || article.markdownFile;\n  const markdownPath = join(archivePath, outputFileName);\n\n  console.log(`\\n📥 Downloading ${article.title} (${article.version})`);\n  console.log('='.repeat(70));\n  console.log(`   URL: ${article.url}`);\n  console.log(`   Target: ${markdownPath}`);\n\n  // Ensure archive directory exists\n  if (!existsSync(archivePath)) {\n    mkdirSync(archivePath, { recursive: true });\n    console.log(`   Created directory: ${archivePath}`);\n  }\n\n  // Extract content from web page\n  console.log('   Extracting content from web page...');\n  const content = await extractArticleContent(article, options.verbose);\n\n  if (!content) {\n    console.error('   ❌ Failed to extract article content');\n    return { success: false, error: 'Failed to extract content' };\n  }\n\n  console.log(`   ✅ Extracted ${content.elements.length} elements`);\n\n  // Convert to markdown\n  console.log('   Converting to markdown...');\n  const markdown = contentToMarkdown(content, article);\n\n  if (options.dryRun) {\n    console.log('   [DRY RUN] Would save markdown file');\n    console.log(`   Preview (first 500 chars):\\n${markdown.substring(0, 500)}...`);\n    return { success: true, dryRun: true };\n  }\n\n  // Save markdown file\n  writeFileSync(markdownPath, markdown, 'utf-8');\n  console.log(`   ✅ Saved ${markdownPath}`);\n  console.log(`   File size: ${(markdown.length / 1024).toFixed(1)} KB`);\n\n  return { success: true, path: markdownPath, size: markdown.length };\n}\n\n/**\n * Main function\n */\nasync function main() {\n  const options = parseArgs();\n\n  // Show process.exit(0);\n  }\n\n  // Get articles to process\n  let articles = [];\n  if (options.all) {\n    articles = getAllArticles();\n  } else if (options.version) {\n    articles = result.success ? } catch (e) { /* ignore */ }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Also hide any fixed/sticky overlays that might cover content\n    const fixedEls = document.querySelectorAll('[style*=\"position: fixed\"], [style*=\"position:fixed\"]');\n    for (const el of fixedEls) {\n      // Only hide if it looks like a popup/overlay (not the main content)\n      const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();\n      if (rect.width > 200 && rect.height > 100) {\n        // Check if it's a popup-like element (covers significant area)\n        const isOverlay = el.classList.toString().match(/popup|modal|overlay|banner|cookie|notification/i);\n        if (isOverlay) {\n          el.style.display = 'none';\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Set Habr theme (light or dark)\n */\nasync function setTheme(page, theme) {\n  await page.evaluate((desiredTheme) => {\n    // Habr uses a theme attribute on the html or body element\n    // Try setting it via CSS class or data attribute\n    const html = document.documentElement;\n    const body = document.body;\n\n    if (desiredTheme === 'dark') {\n      html.classList.add('tm-page-theme_dark');\n      html.classList.remove('tm-page-theme_light');\n      body.classList.add('tm-page-theme_dark');\n      body.classList.remove('tm-page-theme_light');\n      // Also try setting color scheme\n      html.style.colorScheme = 'dark';\n    } else {\n      html.classList.add('tm-page-theme_light');\n      html.classList.remove('tm-page-theme_dark');\n      body.classList.add('tm-page-theme_light');\n      body.classList.remove('tm-page-theme_dark');\n      html.style.colorScheme = 'light';\n    }\n  }, theme);\n\n  // Wait for theme styles to apply\n  await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n}\n\n/**\n * Prepare page for screenshot: scroll to load content, close popups, set theme\n */\nasync function preparePage(page, theme) {\n  // Multi-pass scrolling to load all lazy content\n  for (let pass = 0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36'\n  });\n  const page = await context.newPage();\n\n  console.log('   Navigating to article...');\n  await page.goto(article.url, {\n    waitUntil: 'networkidle',\n    timeout: 120000\n  });\n\n  // Capture light theme screenshot\n  const lightPath = join(archivePath, article.screenshotLightFile || 'article-light.png');\n  console.log(`   Target (light): ${lightPath}`);\n  await captureScreenshotWithTheme(page, lightPath, 'light');\n\n  // Capture dark theme screenshot\n  const darkPath = join(archivePath, article.screenshotDarkFile || 'article-dark.png');\n  console.log(`   Target (dark): ${darkPath}`);\n  await captureScreenshotWithTheme(page, darkPath, 'dark');\n\n  // Also save a default article.png (light theme copy) for backward compatibility\n  const defaultPath = join(archivePath, article.screenshotFile);\n  fs.copyFileSync(lightPath, defaultPath);\n  console.log(`   ✅ Default screenshot (light copy): ${defaultPath}`);\n\n  await browser.close();\n}"

    "newString": "/**\n * Close any popup overlays/modals on the page\n */\nasync function closePopups(page) {\n  await page.evaluate(() => {\n    // Common popup/modal close buttons on Habr\n    const closeSelectors = [\n      // Cookie consent\n      '.tm-cookie-banner__close',\n      '.cookie-banner__close',\n      '[data-test-id=\"cookie-banner-close\"]',\n      // Generic close buttons\n      '.tm-popup__close',\n      '.popup__close',\n      '.modal__close',\n      // Overlay close\n      '.overlay__close',\n      // Any visible close button with aria-label\n      '[aria-label=\"Close\"]',\n      '[aria-label=\"Закрыть\"]',\n      // Dismiss buttons\n      '.tm-base-modal__close',\n      // Notification popups\n      '.tm-notification__close',\n    ];\n\n    for (const selector of closeSelectors) {\n      const els = document.querySelectorAll(selector);\n      for (const el of els) {\n        try { el.click(); } catch (e) { /* ignore */ }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Also hide any fixed/sticky overlays that might cover content\n    const fixedEls = document.querySelectorAll('[style*=\"position: fixed\"], [style*=\"position:fixed\"]');\n    for (const el of fixedEls) {\n      // Only hide if it looks like a popup/overlay (not the main content)\n      const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();\n      if (rect.width > 200 && rect.height > 100) {\n        // Check if it's a popup-like element (covers significant area)\n        const isOverlay = el.classList.toString().match(/popup|modal|overlay|banner|cookie|notification/i);\n        if (isOverlay) {\n          el.style.display = 'none';\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Set Habr theme (light or dark)\n */\nasync function setTheme(page, theme) {\n  await page.evaluate((desiredTheme) => {\n    // Habr uses a theme attribute on the html or body element\n    // Try setting it via CSS class or data attribute\n    const html = document.documentElement;\n    const body = document.body;\n\n    if (desiredTheme === 'dark') {\n      html.classList.add('tm-page-theme_dark');\n      html.classList.remove('tm-page-theme_light');\n      body.classList.add('tm-page-theme_dark');\n      body.classList.remove('tm-page-theme_light');\n      // Also try setting color scheme\n      html.style.colorScheme = 'dark';\n    } else {\n      html.classList.add('tm-page-theme_light');\n      html.classList.remove('tm-page-theme_dark');\n      body.classList.add('tm-page-theme_light');\n      body.classList.remove('tm-page-theme_dark');\n      html.style.colorScheme = 'light';\n    }\n  }, theme);\n\n  // Wait for theme styles to apply\n  await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n}\n\n/**\n * Prepare page for screenshot: scroll to load content, close popups, set theme\n */\nasync function preparePage(page, theme) {\n  // Multi-pass scrolling to load all lazy content\n  for (let pass = 0; pass < 3; pass++) {\n    await page.evaluate(async () => {\n      const scrollHeight = document.documentElement.scrollHeight;\n      const viewportHeight = window.innerHeight;\n      const scrollSteps = Math.ceil(scrollHeight / viewportHeight);\n      for (let i = 0; i < scrollSteps; i++) {\n        window.scrollTo(0, i * viewportHeight);\n        await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 200));\n      }\n      window.scrollTo(0, 0);\n    });\n    await page.waitForTimeout(1000);\n  }\n\n  // Wait for images to load\n  await page.waitForTimeout(3000);\n\n  // Close all popups\n  await closePopups(page);\n\n  // Set theme\n  await setTheme(page, theme);\n\n  // Close popups again in case theme change triggered new ones\n  await closePopups(page);\n\n  // Scroll to top for screenshot\n  await page.evaluate(() => window.scrollTo(0, 0));\n  await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n}\n\n/**\n * Capture a screenshot of the article in a specific theme\n */\nasync function captureScreenshotWithTheme(page, screenshotPath, theme) {\n  console.log(`   Setting ${theme} theme...`);\n  await preparePage(page, theme);\n\n  console.log(`   Taking ${theme} screenshot...`);\n  await page.screenshot({\n    path: screenshotPath,\n    fullPage: true\n  });\n\n  const stats = fs.statSync(screenshotPath);\n  console.log(`   ✅ ${theme} screenshot saved: ${(stats.size / 1024 / 1024).toFixed(1)} MB`);\n}\n\n/**\n * Capture screenshots of the article in both light and dark themes\n */\nasync function captureScreenshot(article) {\n  const archivePath = join(ROOT_DIR, article.archivePath);\n\n  console.log(`\\n📸 Capturing screenshots for ${article.title} (${article.version})`);\n  console.log(`   URL: ${article.url}`);\n\n  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });\n  const context = await browser.newContext({\n    viewport: { width: 1920, height: 1080 },\n    userAgent: 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0;
